steps.m: Three Arabic letters: Alif, Lam, Ra
steps.n: These are three separate Arabic letters written individually
These letters appear at the start of some chapters of the Quran. While we can read them, their deeper meaning is known only to Allah.
This is the basic feminine demonstrative pronoun used to point to something
Adding ل with sukun shows the thing being pointed to is farther away
Adding كَ at the end completes the demonstrative pronoun. In this ayah, it's pointing to the verses (ءَايٰتُ) that follow.
The root letters combine with a long 'aa' sound and 'ة' at the end to make a noun meaning 'sign'
Changed to plural by adding ات and stretching the first vowel
The damma shows this word is the subject being pointed to by تِلْكَ (those)
Changed from action to the result of that action (like changing 'write' to 'writing/book')
Added ٱلْ to show this is a specific book being referred to

steps.m: of the book (describes what ءَايٰتُ/verses are of)
steps.n: Changed the ending to show this is 'of the book' - it tells us what the verses belong to
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show something that does the action of making clear. This describes the Book (الكتاب) mentioned in the ayah.
Added ال to make it definite - now specifically referring to 'the Book that makes clear'
Added -i sound at the end to show it belongs to or describes something (the Book)
This is a particle that adds emphasis to a statement, like saying 'indeed' or 'certainly'

steps.m: indeed we (subject of أَنزَلْنٰهُ - we sent it down)
steps.n: The long alif (آ) at the end shows this is about 'we/us'. This word connects to the next word أَنزَلْنٰهُ (we sent it down) where 'we' is doing the action.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: to cause to descend, to send down
steps.n: Adding أ (hamza) at the start makes it mean 'to make something go down' instead of just 'going down' - like the difference between 'drop' and 'make drop'
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action of sending down
The ه at the end means 'it' - the thing that was sent down. This word is a complete verbal phrase and is not related to other words through case endings.

steps.m: to read or recite something out loud

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changes the verb 'read' into a noun meaning 'that which is read intensively'

steps.m: a recitation (object of أَنزَلْنٰهُ - we sent it down)
steps.n: Added tanwin alif (ًا) at the end to show this is the object being sent down in the phrase 'We sent it down as a recitation'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic noun
The doubled ي at the end shows this describes something connected to Arabs or their speech (like English '-ic' in 'Arabic')
The ًا ending shows this word describes how the Quran (قُرْءٰنًا) was sent down - it was sent down as an Arabic recitation

steps.m: surely perhaps/so that (introduces emphasized hope or expectation)
steps.n: Adding the shadda on the first letter makes the meaning stronger, like saying 'surely' or 'definitely'

steps.m: so that you all (connects to تَعْقِلُونَ - you all understand)
steps.n: The كُمْ at the end shows this is talking to multiple people. This word connects to تَعْقِلُونَ (you all understand) in the next part of the ayah, showing why something was done - in this case, why the Quran was sent down in Arabic.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he' to 'you' and past to present by adding تَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: you all use reason (addressing the listeners of the Quran)
steps.n: The ون ending shows this is addressing multiple people. In this ayah, it's specifically addressing those who are being told about the Arabic Quran.
This is a standalone pronoun meaning 'we'. In this ayah, it's used to show that Allah is the one telling/narrating the story, as نَحْنُ (we) is the subject of نَقُصُّ (narrate/tell).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he told' to command 'tell!' by changing vowels and doubling middle letter
Added نَ at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action. This word follows نَحْنُ (we) in the ayah to emphasize that it is Allah who is telling the story.
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'to'
The ى changes to يْ before adding a pronoun suffix

steps.m: upon you/to you (speaking to one male)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show this is happening to 'you' (when speaking to one male)
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أ at start changes it from 'being good' to 'making something good'

steps.m: to tell a story, to follow tracks

steps.n: Adding the 'a' sounds (fathas) turns the root letters into 'he told a story' in the past
Added ال ('the') to make it specific, and changed the ending to show it's receiving the action of being 'best'
This is the basic interrogative/relative pronoun meaning 'what'
Added the preposition بِ to show this describes how something was done. Here it connects to the verb أَوْحَيْنَآ (We revealed), showing what We revealed with/by means of.
The ا is elongated to آ for pronunciation emphasis at the end of the word

steps.m: to reveal or inspire something to someone

steps.n: Root letters were connected with short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make past tense 'he revealed'
Added أَ at the beginning to show causing something to happen. The ي changes to ى because it's at the end.
The ى changes back to يْ before adding نَا
Added نَآ to show 'we' did the action. The long alif at the end shows emphasis.
This is a preposition that shows direction or movement towards something
The ى changes to ي with sukun when connecting to pronouns
Added كَ at the end to show this is directed to one male person being spoken to
Basic pointing word that draws attention to something
Added ذا to specify pointing to something masculine and nearby
Added alif with overbar (ٰ) and fatha marks to show proper pronunciation. In this ayah, it's pointing to the Quran which is mentioned right after it.

steps.m: to read or recite something carefully

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: recitation, that which is read abundantly
steps.n: Changes verb 'read' into noun meaning 'that which is read/recited' with emphasis on abundance
Added ال to make it specific to THE sacred text (the Quran)

steps.m: the Quran (object of نَقُصُّ - we relate)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb نَقُصُّ (we relate)
This is a conditional particle that shows something depends on something else, like saying 'if you study, you will learn'
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it
Connected the root letters with fathas to make the basic past tense verb

Changed to 'you' form by: 1) Adding تَ at the end to show 'you', 2) Changing first vowel to ُ and middle to ْ because و drops between two vowels, 3) This word appears in وَإِن كُنتَ (and indeed you were)
This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects with قَبْلِهِۦ (before it) to show the time period being talked about.
Added vowels to make a noun meaning 'before' that can show when something happened
Adds هِ to show it refers back to the Quran (القُرْءَانَ) mentioned earlier in the verse
Added a fatha at the end to connect it to the next word
Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, showing this person was definitely/surely from the unaware ones mentioned next (الْغٰفِلِينَ)
Added ا after first letter and ِ after second letter to show someone who does the action. This creates the active participle (doer) form.
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific. The long ا is written as ٰ (dagger alif) in this position.
Added ين at the end to make it plural (more than one). This form is used when the word follows certain words like مِن (from/of).
Added َ at the end because this word follows كُنتَ (you were) in the sentence "you were among the unaware ones"

steps.m: When (introducing Yusuf's statement to his father)
steps.n: This word introduces a past event or memory. Here it's used to start telling about when Yusuf spoke to his father about his dream.
Added fathas to show this is past tense and means 'he did it'
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs
This is the basic form of the proper name Yusuf (Joseph), which comes from Hebrew יוֹסֵף (Yosef)

steps.m: Joseph (as the subject who is speaking to his father)
steps.n: Added damma marks (ُ) to show this is the subject doing the action of speaking (قَالَ) to his father
Connected the three root letters أ-ب-و to make the basic word for 'father'
Added لِ at the beginning to show direction/purpose
Added ي at the end to show possession (meaning 'my')
Added هِ at the end to show possession (his). This word is the indirect object of the verb قَالَ (said) in this ayah, showing who Yusuf was speaking to.
Connected the letters and removed the weak letter و to form the basic noun 'father'
Added ت at the end to make it feminine, like when a daughter calls her father
Added kasra at the end to show we are calling out to father, like saying 'O father!'

steps.m: O my father (addressing Yusuf's father in the ayah)
steps.n: Added يٰٓ at the beginning to call out to someone, like saying 'Hey!' or 'O!' in English. This is Yusuf speaking to his father in this ayah.
Added shadda to strengthen the meaning. This particle is used at the start of sentences to emphasize what comes after it.

steps.m: Indeed I (speaking to أَبِيهِ - his father)
steps.n: Added ى at the end to show that Yusuf is speaking about himself to his father. This makes it personal - 'Indeed I' instead of just 'Indeed'.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic verb meaning
Adding sukun (ْ) on the last letter makes it a complete past tense verb
Added تُ at the end to show that 'I' did the action in the past
Adding basic vowel marks to make the word readable
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. This word combines with أَحَدَ (one) before it to form أَحَدَ عَشَرَ (eleven) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters together with vowel marks. The َ (fatha) on first letter and ْ (sukun) on second letter create the basic noun pattern.
The ًا at the end shows this is the object being seen in Yusuf's statement 'I saw (رَأَيْتُ) eleven stars.' The tanween (double mark) shows it's indefinite (a star, not the star).
Changed from action of shining to the noun 'sun'
Added 'the' to make it specific
Added mark to show this is what was seen in the dream (object of رَأَيْتُ - I saw)

steps.m: and the sun (as object of رَأَيْتُ - I saw)
steps.n: Added 'and' to connect it with the previous things Yusuf saw in his dream
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic noun
Added و at beginning to connect this word to الشمس (the sun) mentioned before it in the ayah
Added ال (the) to make it 'the moon' instead of just any moon
Added fatha at the end because this moon is what Yusuf saw - it's the object of the verb رَأَيْتُ (I saw)

steps.m: to see or perceive with eyes or mind

steps.n: Changed from basic root 'to see' into past tense 'he saw' by adding vowel marks and writing ى at the end
Changed ى back to يْ because more letters will be added after it
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did the action
Added هُمْ at the end to show who was seen
Added kasra to show this word connects to what comes after it

steps.m: to/for me (connected to رَأَيْتُ - I saw)
steps.n: Added ى at the end to show this means 'me'. This word connects to رَأَيْتُ (I saw) in this ayah, showing what was seen appeared to/for the speaker.

steps.m: to prostrate, to bow down in worship
one who prostrates (object of رَأَيْتُ - I saw)
steps.n: This form shows who does the action. The word is being acted upon by رَأَيْتُ (I saw) in Yusuf's dream.

steps.m: prostrating ones (object of رَأَيْتُ - I saw)
steps.n: The ين ending shows this is plural (more than two) and masculine. It's in the accusative case because it's the object of رَأَيْتُ (I saw).
Added fathas to make it past tense. This shows that someone (he) did this action in the past.
Changed و to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter)

steps.m: to build or raise up (including raising children)

steps.n: Root meaning 'to build' becomes 'son' - like how parents build/raise their children
Added يَّ (ya with shadda) at the end to make it mean 'my little son' - shows both possession and affection

steps.m: O my son (being addressed by يَعْقُوبُ)
steps.n: Added يٰ at beginning to show direct address. This word is being used by Prophet Yaqub to address his son Yusuf.
This is a negative particle used to say 'no' or 'don't'
Adding the fatha makes this a command negation. Here it's telling Ya'qub's son not to tell his dream to his brothers.

steps.m: to tell a story or narrate events

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense with 'you' as subject. The sukun at the end shows this is a command form, telling Yusuf not to tell his dream.
Changes from verb 'to see' into noun 'dream/vision'
Adds 'your' to show it's your dream. This is what is being told/narrated in the sentence.
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or upon something else. Here it connects to the word إِخْوَتِكَ (your brothers) to show the dream shouldn't be told upon/to them.
Added an alif maksura (ى with a small alif on top) which is a common way to write this word in the Quran
Added a small vertical line (ٓ) above the word which helps with Quranic recitation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic singular noun
Added إِ at start and ة at end to make the plural form
Adds كَ to show possession (your). This word follows the preposition عَلَىٰٓ (upon) in the ayah.
Added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning the act of plotting
Changed from the noun 'plotting' to a command meaning 'plot!' by changing the vowel from 'a' to 'i'
Added يَ at beginning to make it present tense
Added وا to make it plural (they)

steps.m: and so they plot (connected to لَا تَقْصُصْ - do not tell)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to show this is a result of the previous action. The small circle (۟) indicates end of word.
Connected the two basic parts: ل (for/to) and ك (you)

steps.m: for you (object of يَكِيدُوا - they plot)
steps.n: Added fatha marks to show clear pronunciation. This word shows who they might plot against in the ayah.
Changed from verb 'to plot' into the noun 'a plot' by adding vowels to the root letters

steps.m: a plot/scheme (as the object being made against يٰبُنَىَّ)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being made against the son. This connects to the earlier part of the ayah where the father is warning his son about his brothers plotting against him.
This is the basic form of the emphasis particle that makes statements stronger
Added kasra (ِ) under the first letter to help with pronunciation
Added shadda (ّ) to make the emphasis stronger. This word emphasizes that 'Satan' (الشَّيْطٰنَ) which comes after it is definitely an enemy.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added ي after first letter and ا after second letter to create an intensive form showing someone who embodies this quality
Added ال (with hamza wasl ٱ) to make it definite, and doubled the ش with shadda because it comes after ال
Added fatha at the end because this word is the object of إِنَّ. Also changed alif to alif madd (ٰ) for proper writing

steps.m: to be close and familiar with others

steps.n: Root meaning 'being social' became a noun meaning all humans (since humans are social beings)
Adding ان makes it refer to a single human with all human qualities
Adding ال makes it specific
Added لِ to show this is who something is for
Changed the alif to a long alif (ٰ) and added kasra at the end to show this word is affected by something else in the sentence

steps.m: to be hostile, to cross boundaries against someone

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to be hostile' into noun 'enemy' by adding specific vowel pattern
Adding double damma makes it 'an enemy' instead of just 'enemy'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show this describes someone/something that makes things clear
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning
Added the -un ending to show this is describing 'enemy' as being clear/obvious
This is the basic demonstrative word meaning 'that'
Added ل to give more emphasis to the pointing word
Added كَ at beginning and end to show comparison - 'like/similar to that'
Added و at beginning to connect this phrase to what follows - 'and thus your Lord will choose you'
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Added ا at start and ت in middle to show doing the action for oneself - like picking something special for yourself
Changed from past tense (chose) to present tense (chooses) by adding يَ at the start and changing the ending

steps.m: he specially selects/chooses you (object of رَبُّكَ/your Lord)
steps.n: Added second person singular object pronoun suffix ك. This verb يَجْتَبِيكَ shows how your Lord (رَبُّكَ) specially chooses you.

steps.m: to care for, raise, develop gradually

steps.n: The middle letter gets doubled (shadda) to show intensity of the caring/nurturing quality
Adding damma to show this is the doer of the verb يَجْتَبِيكَ (chooses you)
Adding second person singular possessive suffix. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb يَجْتَبِيكَ (chooses you).
This form turns the action of knowing into the noun knowledge
Root letters got vowels (fatha) added to make past tense verb 'he knew/taught'
Doubled middle letter to show intensive teaching/instruction
Changes from past to present tense by adding يُ at the start and changing the vowels
Adds و (and) to the beginning of the word, connecting it to what came before

steps.m: and he teaches you (object of يَجْتَبِيكَ)

steps.n: Basic preposition showing where the teaching comes from - it connects 'He teaches you' (يُعَلِّمُكَ) with 'the interpretation' (تَأْوِيلِ). The sukun at the end shows it's connected to what comes after it.

steps.m: to return something to its original state or explain its deeper meaning
steps.n: The doubled و shows emphasis on being first
Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to make it into a past tense verb meaning 'he interpreted'
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'the act of interpreting' by adding تَ at the start and ي after the second root letter
Added kasra at the end to show this word owns or describes what comes after it. Here it's connected to الْأَحَادِيثِ meaning 'interpretation of the narratives'
Basic meaning of occurring or happening
Changes from a verb (to tell) into a noun (the thing being told)
Changes from single story to multiple stories by adding أَ at the start - a broken plural pattern
Added ال for definiteness and ِ to show this word is being acted upon by تَأْوِيلِ (interpretation of)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he completed' to a command 'complete!' by adding shadda and dropping the last letter
Added يُ at start to make it 'he' doing the action in present tense, and doubled the م (meem) to show doing something completely/fully

steps.m: and he completes (fully) (connected to يَجْتَبِيكَ - chooses you)
steps.n: Added conjunction وَ to connect this action with the previous verb in the ayah (يَجْتَبِيكَ - chooses you)
Added vowel marks to create the basic noun form
Adds ة (ta marbuta) at the end to make it mean 'a blessing' or 'a favor' instead of just 'blessing' in general

steps.m: His blessing (object of يُتِمُّ - he completes)
steps.n: Adds the third person masculine singular possessive pronoun 'his'. The taa marbouta becomes taa maftuha before the suffix. This word is the direct object (what is being completed) of the verb يُتِمُّ (he completes).
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
When adding a pronoun after على, the ى changes to يْ
Added the pronoun كَ meaning 'you' to show who is being acted upon. This word connects to يُتِمُّ (completes) in the ayah.
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation of basic preposition
Added alif khanjariyah (small alif) to show this is the full form of the word
Added vertical fatḥah (ٓ) to show elongation of final sound
Added conjunction و with fatha to connect this word to what came before it

steps.m: to lead, be first, return to
one who leads or comes first
steps.n: Changed from root meaning 'to lead' into a word meaning 'first' by adding the vowel marks 'fatha' and 'sukoon'
The و changes to ا and the hamza changes to ء. This is a special word meaning 'family' or 'household' that comes from the idea of those who follow or are led by someone.
Added kasra at the end to show this word belongs to or is connected to what comes after it (يَعْقُوبَ/Jacob)
This is a proper name that doesn't come from an Arabic root. It's the Hebrew name Ya'aqov (יַעֲקֹב) which entered Arabic as يَعْقُوب.
Added fatha at the end because this name is part of a list after إِبْرٰهِيمَ (Abraham), showing it's being acted upon in the same way.
Starting with the comparison particle ك which shows similarity
Added ما to make the comparison more general and complete
The alif at the end is written with a long آ to show proper pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Combined the doubled letters with a shadda mark

steps.m: he made complete/caused to be complete
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning to show causing something to happen

steps.m: he completed it (referring to نِعْمَتَهُ - His favor)
steps.n: Added هَا at the end to show what is being completed (referring back to the favor/blessing mentioned earlier in the verse)
Basic preposition showing something is on top of or upon something else
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) which is just a spelling variation of ى at the end of words

steps.m: upon/on (connected to ءَالِ يَعْقُوبَ - the family of Jacob)
steps.n: Added a small alif (ٓ) above to show this word connects to what comes after it - here it connects to 'the family of Jacob'
Connected the letters and added basic vowels to make the word pronounceable
Changed أَبَو to أَبَيْ and added ك at the end to mean 'your father' (when speaking to one male)
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or a part of something. Here it shows Yusuf will learn some of the interpretation of events.
Root letters form a simple word meaning 'before' (like 'pre-' in English)
Adds damma to show this word is in the nominative case
This is a proper name that comes from Hebrew/Aramaic 'Abraham' meaning 'father of many'. It doesn't come from an Arabic root.

steps.m: Abraham (object of أَتَمَّهَا - He completed it)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this name is the object of the verb أَتَمَّهَا (He completed it) in this ayah, along with يَعْقُوبَ (Jacob).
This is the basic proper name Isaac, with vowel marks added to show pronunciation
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the verb أَتَمَّهَا (He completed it)
Added و at the beginning to connect this name to the previous one (إبراهيم)

This is a special emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger and more certain. Here it emphasizes that 'your Lord' (رَبَّكَ) is wise and knowing. The shadda (ّ) makes the 'n' sound stronger to show extra emphasis.
Added fatha on first letter and doubled the middle letter to show complete mastery and care
Added ك to show possession (your). This word is doing the action of choosing (يَجْتَبِيكَ) in this verse.
Connected the root letters with vowels to form the basic past tense verb

Added ي between second and third root letters and tanween damma at the end to create an intensive form showing complete mastery of knowledge. This word describes Allah's perfect knowledge as mentioned in relation to رَبَّكَ (your Lord) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Forms an intensive adjective showing someone who possesses great wisdom. This word describes Allah (رَبَّكَ - your Lord) in this ayah.
This is a basic particle that shows something definitely happened in the past
Added ل at the beginning to make the meaning stronger, like adding 'surely' or 'definitely' in English
Added shadda on ل to make the emphasis even stronger. This word emphasizes the verb كَانَ (there was) that comes after it in the ayah.
Added fathas to make it past tense. This shows someone or something existed in the past.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic words with و in the middle.
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment. The kasra is the standard voweling for this preposition.
Added kasra (ِ) to show this is a preposition meaning 'in'
Added ى at the end which is standard spelling for this preposition. This word connects to يُوسُفَ (Yusuf) to show what something is in.
This is a proper name, likely of Hebrew origin, referring to Prophet Joseph. The damma marks (ُ) help specify the correct pronunciation.
The fatha (َ) at the end shows Yusuf is being mentioned as the direct object - someone is doing something to/about him
Root meaning 'family ties' becomes the noun 'brother' - like how a brother is your closest family member
Adding alif at start makes it a clear noun form
Adding ة makes it refer to a group of brothers
Added conjunction 'and'
Added -i ending to show it belongs to someone
Added 'his' pronoun suffix with elongation mark to show possession by Yusuf
The root letters combine and add ة to make a noun meaning 'something that shows or points to something else'
Added ات to make it plural
Added tanween damma to show this is the subject of كَانَ (was) in this ayah. The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this form.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun describing someone who does the action
Made the word plural to show many people who ask
Added 'the' to make it specific and doubled the س with shadda
Added لِ at the beginning to show 'for' or 'to'

steps.m: for the ones who ask (with emphasis)
steps.n: The لِ gets a shadda to emphasize 'for' and blend with the next letter

steps.m: when (referring to a past time, introducing قَالُوا - they said)
steps.n: This is a time particle that introduces a past event. In this ayah, it introduces what the brothers said about Yusuf.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
The middle letter و changes to ا because it has a fatha before and after it. This makes the word easier to pronounce.
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (Yusuf's brothers) are doing the action
This is a proper noun name
The ُ ending shows this is the subject (doer) in the sentence
The لَ adds emphasis, showing this is definitely true. In this ayah, Yusuf is the subject of أَحَبُّ (more beloved).
Added vowel marks to make the word mean 'brother' - this is the basic form used when saying 'brother of'
Added هُ meaning 'his'. This word joins with يُوسُفُ as the subjects that are أَحَبُّ (more beloved) to their father.
The وَ means 'and'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Adding shadda doubles the middle root letter ب to intensify meaning
The prefix أ changes حَبَّ (loved) into أَحَبّ (more loved/beloved), making it a comparison word - like adding 'more' in English
Added damma at the end because this word is the subject (doer) in the sentence
This is a preposition showing direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects to أَبِينَا (our father) to show who is more beloved to.
Added an alif symbol (ٰ) above the ى to show it's a long vowel sound
Added a small alif (ٓ) above to indicate a slight lengthening of the final sound when connecting to the next word
Connected the root letters أ-ب-و with vowels to make the basic word for 'father'
Changed the ending vowel to kasra to prepare for showing possession
Added ي at the end to mean 'my'
Added نَا meaning 'our'. This word follows إِلَىٰٓ (to) in the ayah, showing who the father belongs to.
Basic preposition meaning 'from' or 'of'
Added a double letter (shadda) to connect with نا that comes after it
Added نا at the end to show this means 'us'
This is a personal pronoun meaning 'we'. The ُ (damma) at the end shows it's doing something in the sentence.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. In this ayah, it connects to the previous statement about Yusuf and his brother.
Added vowels to make a noun meaning 'group' from the concept of joining together
Added ة to specify one particular group
Added tanween to show this is the main subject. In this ayah, the brothers are calling themselves a strong group.
Adding shadda (ّ) on the ن changes this from a conditional word to an emphasis word. In this ayah, it emphasizes that the brothers are saying their father is 'surely' in clear error.
Root letters أ-ب-و joined together with vowels to make the word 'father'
Changed to accusative case because it comes after إِنَّ, which makes nouns after it accusative (like the object of an action)
Added نَا at the end to show it belongs to us (the speakers)
Basic preposition meaning 'in'. This word has no root since it's a particle.

steps.m: surely in (emphasizing that يوسف is in ضَلٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ/clear error)
steps.n: The لَ adds emphasis to show that something is definitely true. In this ayah, it's emphasizing that Yusuf is truly/definitely in clear error according to his brothers.

steps.m: to wander off the right path

steps.n: Adding the short 'a' vowels (fatHa) turns the root into 'he went astray' in the past tense
Adding alif makes the meaning stronger, showing someone is really lost

steps.m: in a state of complete error (object of فِى)
steps.n: The word changed to show it comes after فِى (in/within) by getting a different ending with the alif (ٰ) and kasra marks (ٍ)
Added a shadda (double letter) and vowels to make it past tense - 'he made clear'
Changed from past tense verb 'made clear' to active participle 'making clear' by adding mu- prefix and changing vowels
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he killed' to 'kill!' - a command telling one person to do it
Adds plural masculine marker to make command for group
This is the basic form of the proper name Yusuf, borrowed from Hebrew (יוֹסֵף‎, Yosef). In Arabic, it's treated as a diptote (doesn't take tanween).

steps.m: Joseph (as the object being acted upon in اقْتُلُوا يُوسُفَ - kill Joseph)
steps.n: The fatḥa (َ) at the end shows that Yusuf is the object being acted upon by the verb اقْتُلُوا (kill).
Basic connecting word that gives a choice between two options: 'kill Joseph' or 'throw him'
Added fatha (َ) on أ to show how to pronounce it correctly
Added kasra (ِ) at the end because it's followed by another word starting with a hamza (ٱطْرَحُوهُ)
This is the basic concept of throwing or casting something away
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changed from 'he threw' to 'throw!' by adding i- at the start and dropping the last vowel - this makes it a command to one person
Changed from singular to plural command by adding وا
Added هُ to show who is being thrown away, and changed initial alif to waṣla form
Connected the letters together with basic vowel marks to make it readable

steps.m: a land (as the destination of طْرَحُوهُ/throw him)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the destination where they want to throw Yusuf. This connects to the verb طْرَحُوهُ (throw him) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make past tense verb
Changed to present/future tense by adding يَ at start, sukun on middle letter, and damma at end
The و at the end is dropped because this word comes after a command and shows what will happen - when you want to show what happens after a command, you drop the last letter
Added fatha to show how the letter is pronounced
Added ك with damma to show it refers to multiple people being addressed

steps.m: for you all (receiving the action of يَخْلُ - become clear/empty)
steps.n: Added م with sukun to complete the plural 'you' ending. This word shows who will receive the benefit of the father's attention becoming clear/empty (يَخْلُ)

steps.m: to face or turn towards something

Added damma at the end to show this is the first part of a possession relationship with 'your father' (أَبِيكُمْ) coming next in the ayah
Root letters أ-ب-و become the word 'father' by adding the vowel mark fatHa (َ) on top of أ
Added kasra and ي to show possession relationship
Added كُمْ to show the father belongs to multiple people being addressed
Added short vowels (fat-ha) to make it past tense 'he became'
Removed middle letter و and changed vowels to make it mean 'become' in present tense instead of past tense
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' are doing the action
Added وا at end to show multiple people (you all) are doing the action

steps.m: and you all become (after killing/banishing يُوسُفَ)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at beginning to connect this action with the previous actions in the ayah about killing or banishing Yusuf
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from, like saying 'from' in English
Added the vowel mark ِ (kasra) under the م to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'min'
Added a small ن mark (called tanwīn) after the ن to show it connects smoothly to the next word بَعْدِهِۦ (after him)

steps.m: to be after, to come later

steps.n: Root letters ب-ع-د become the word 'after' by adding vowel marks
Added kasra at the end because this word follows the preposition مِنۢ (from/after)
Added هِ (him) with the small و (ۦ) mark that shows this is pronounced clearly at the end

steps.m: people who stand together (as a group)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun. The root meaning of standing together became associated with a group of people who unite as one.
Added tanween alif to show this word is receiving the action of killing in the phrase 'kill a people'

steps.m: to make something good or right

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to active participle form to describe someone who has this quality
Added plural ending ين to show multiple righteous people. This is the object of the verb تَكُونُوا (you become) in this ayah.
Added the fatha mark to show this is the object (accusative case)
Added vowel marks to make the basic past tense form. This word is related to قَآئِلٌ (a sayer) that comes right after it in the ayah.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic words with و in the middle.
Root letters 'q-w-l' get vowels 'a' added between them to make the past tense 'he said'
Adding ا after first letter turns verb into noun meaning 'person who does the action'
Lengthening the ا to آ for proper pronunciation
Added tanween to show this is indefinite (like saying 'a speaker' instead of 'the speaker'). This word tells us who did the speaking mentioned in قَالَ (said).
This is a basic preposition showing something comes from somewhere or someone. In this ayah, it's used with a speaker from among a group.
The م gets doubled (shadda) because it comes right after a word ending in ن or ل
Added هُمْ to show this refers to 'them' (masculine plural) - referring to the group speaking about Yusuf
This is a negative particle that tells people not to do something. Here it's telling them not to kill (تَقْتُلُوا۟) Yusuf.
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
The تَ at the start shows it's 'you' doing the action
The وا shows multiple people are being addressed. This word appears after لا which makes it a negative command - 'do not kill'
This is a proper name borrowed from ancient Hebrew (יוֹסֵף/Yosef), referring to the prophet, son of Yaqub (Jacob)

steps.m: Yusuf/Joseph (as the object of تَقْتُلُوا - do not kill)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows that Yusuf is the one being affected by the action (the brothers are discussing not killing him)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the start makes the action cause something to happen. This is a verb, so it doesn't have case endings like nouns do.
This makes it a command to multiple people
Added هُ to show who is being thrown (referring to يُوسُفَ/Yusuf from the ayah)
Added وَ (and) to connect this to the previous suggestion in the conversation about what to do with Yusuf

steps.m: to disappear from sight, be hidden

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Changed the verb into a noun for a specific place where something can be hidden

steps.m: of the hidden place/depth (in the well)

The doubled middle letter shows this is a deep pit, not just any hole
Adding kasra shows this word is being acted upon by the preposition فِى (in) from the ayah

steps.m: of/in the deep well/pit (where they planned to put يُوسُفَ)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it refer to the specific well they were discussing for putting Yusuf in

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

Added plural ending ين to show multiple doers. This is the predicate of كُنتُم (you were) in the ayah.

steps.m: to give sincere advice or counsel

Added هُ (meaning 'him') to show who it's for. Like changing 'for' to 'for him'
Added ۥ which is a pronunciation helper mark showing that the هُ should be pronounced clearly

Added fathas to make it past tense. This shows the action happened in the past.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs
This is a special word that makes what comes after it more certain, like adding 'definitely' in English

Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

Changed to present tense with 'you' as subject by adding تَ at start, putting sukun on first root letter, and adding ُ at end
Added وا at the end to show that multiple people are doing the action
This is a preposition that shows how something is done or connected
Added هِ to show this refers to Yusuf (Joseph) who was mentioned earlier in the conversation
Added a small alif (ۦ) after هِ to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: you all (when talking to a group)

steps.m: and you all (while being unaware of يَأْكُلَهُ)

Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
This form shows who does the action. Here it describes the state of أنتم (you all)

steps.m: those who are neglectful/unaware (plural, describing أنتم)

Adding short vowels 'a' after each letter to make it past tense 'he said'

steps.m: surely if (introducing what Jacob's sons said about the wolf)

steps.m: he ate him (the wolf ate يوسف)

steps.m: the wolf (subject of أَكَلَهُ - ate him)

Adding وَ at the start connects this word to what came before it. In this ayah, they're saying 'and we' are a strong group, connecting to their previous statement about the wolf.

Added لَ at beginning to add emphasis

Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis to the timing
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens after something else

steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action, with long alif for emphasis
Basic preposition meaning 'to' or 'towards'
Changed ى to يْ to prepare for connecting with the pronoun

steps.m: to him (connected to وَأَوْحَيْنَآ - and We inspired)
steps.n: Added هِ (him) at the end to show who is being referred to. This word connects to وَأَوْحَيْنَآ (and We inspired) to show who received the inspiration.

steps.m: to tell important news or information

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Adding a double letter (shadda) in the middle makes the meaning stronger, like saying you're really making sure to inform someone well
Added تُ at the start to show that 'you' are doing the action
Added نَّ at the end to show this will definitely happen, like adding an exclamation mark!

steps.m: I swear you will definitely inform
steps.n: Added لَ at the start to show this is a strong promise or oath, like saying 'I swear' in English

steps.m: I swear you will definitely inform them (about this matter)
steps.n: Added هُم at the end to show who is being informed (them). This connects to the next part of the verse where it talks about 'this matter of theirs'
Changed the verb into a noun by adding fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter. This makes it mean the thing itself rather than the action.
Added بِ at the beginning to show how this matter relates to the situation

steps.m: with/about their matter (referring to يَشْعُرُونَ - they perceive)
steps.n: Added هِمْ (their) at the end to show who the matter belongs to. The هِمْ refers to the same people who 'do not perceive' later in the ayah.
This is the basic attention-drawing part that starts many pointing words
Added ذ which makes it specifically point to something
Added ا and fatha to complete the masculine singular demonstrative pronoun
Added diacritics to show proper pronunciation - damma (ُ) shows it's the subject form of 'they', and sukun (ْ) shows the end of the word
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb لَا يَشْعُرُونَ (they do not perceive) in this ayah.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَشْعُرُونَ (they perceive) into 'they do not perceive'. It's one of the most common words used to say 'no' or 'not' in Arabic.
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: to sense or be aware of something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they sense/are sensing (subject of لا in لا يَشْعُرُونَ)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This word comes after لا (not) in the ayah, so it means 'they do not sense'
The root letters ج-ي-أ became جَاءَ (he came) by connecting the letters and adding vowels. The middle letter ي turned into ا, which is common for this type of verb.
Added و at the end to show many people did the action. Changed the last vowel to ُ before و.
Added وَ at beginning to mean 'and'. Added ٓ (elongation mark) to show the long sound at the end.
Changed to أَبَا because it's receiving the action - they came to their father. The alif at the end shows it's the object.
Added هُمْ at the end to show it's their father. The sukun (ْ) on م shows the end of the word.
Changed the verb into a noun for the time period
Added double fatha to show it's indefinite and in accusative case

steps.m: at night (describing when they came to their father)
steps.n: The middle letter is stretched longer to emphasize they came late at night (like saying 'laaate at night')
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final ي changes to ى because it's at the end of the word.
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed middle vowel to sukun, and final vowel to kasra. The ى changes back to ي in present tense.

steps.m: they weep/cry (referring to Joseph's brothers in this ayah)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. The ي at the end is dropped before adding ون. In this ayah, it refers to Joseph's brothers who came to their father crying.
Adding short vowels 'a' to make it mean 'he said' in the past
Changed middle letter و to ا and added وا at the end to show many brothers speaking
Adding نا shows the father belongs to us (our father)
Added calling particle يا with long vowel to show we're calling out to someone

steps.m: O our father (said by Yusuf's brothers to their father)
steps.n: Added نا with long alif to show it's 'our father'. This is being said by Prophet Yusuf's brothers to their father Ya'qub.
Adding a shadda (double letter mark) changes this from meaning 'if' to meaning 'indeed'. This makes what comes after it stronger and more certain.
The suffix ا means 'we'. This word connects to ذَهَبْنَا (we went) in this ayah, showing who is speaking.

steps.m: to go or move away from a place

steps.n: Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to show this happened in the past
Adding نا changes it from 'he went' to 'we went'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make basic past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to show people competing/racing against each other, making it mutual action
Changed to present tense 'we' form by adding ن at start and changing vowels
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Adding نا makes it mean 'we'. This word is the object (what was done) in relation to the verb ذَهَبْنَا (we went).
Adding و connects this to the previous statement about going away. This word is followed by يُوسُفَ showing what they left behind - Yusuf.
This is a Biblical/Quranic proper name that entered Arabic through Semitic languages. It does not follow standard Arabic morphological patterns.
Added the standard vowel marks to show how this name is pronounced in Arabic: 'yu' and 'su'.

steps.m: Joseph (as the object of تَرَكْنَا - we left)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show that Joseph is the object being acted upon in this sentence - specifically, he is the one that 'we left' (تَرَكْنَا).
Connected the root letters that mean being present or existing
Added vowel marks to make it a preposition. In this ayah, it shows where they left Yusuf.

steps.m: to provide temporary benefit or enjoyment

steps.n: Added vowels and alif to make it into a noun meaning things that provide temporary benefit
Added kasra at the end to show this word belongs to/is possessed by something
Added نا at the end to show the belongings belong to us. This word comes after عند (with/at) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters and added vowels to show this happened in the past
Added هُ to show who was eaten. This refers back to Yusuf (يُوسُفَ) mentioned earlier in the verse, when the brothers are making up their story about what happened to him.

steps.m: and so he ate him (referring to يُوسُفَ)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened as a result of what came before - the brothers are explaining what they claim happened after they left Yusuf with their belongings.
The root letters were joined together and given vowels to make the word for 'wolf'. The middle letter became a hamza (ء) on a ya seat.
Added ال to make it specific and shadda on ذ because it comes after ال

steps.m: the wolf (subject of أَكَلَهُ - ate him)
steps.n: Added damma to show this is the subject doing the eating in the phrase فَأَكَلَهُ (and it ate him)

steps.m: not (you are not a believer in us)
steps.n: This is a negative particle that makes the statement 'you are a believer in us' into 'you are not a believer in us'

steps.m: and not (you are not a believer in us)
steps.n: The و prefix adds the meaning 'and' to create a compound negative particle

steps.m: and not (you are not a believer in us)
steps.n: The alif is elongated for emphasis in pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning
Basic personal pronoun form - not derived from a root. Shows who is being talked to.

steps.m: you (masculine singular, being addressed by يُوسُفَ's brothers)
steps.n: This is incorrect. أَنْ and أَنْتَ are completely different words. أَنْ means 'that' while أَنْتَ is the pronoun 'you'. They are not related by adding تَ. أَنْتَ is a standalone pronoun meaning 'you' for one male person.
Adding alif at start changes meaning to 'make someone do the action'
Changed from an action (believing) to describing someone who does that action (believer)

steps.m: believing (in us) (connected to أَنتَ - you are)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show what is being believed in, and added tanween to show it's indefinite. This connects to أَنتَ (you are) earlier in the verse to mean 'you are not believing in us'
Combined the preposition ل (to/for) with نا (us) and added vowel marks to show how it's pronounced

steps.m: to/for us (emphasizing what was said to يٰٓأَبَانَا)
steps.n: Added shadda (ّ) on ل to show extra emphasis since this is part of what the brothers are saying to their father, trying to convince him of their story
This is a conditional particle that introduces a hypothetical situation. In this ayah, it's used to say 'even if we were truthful' - showing the brothers know their father won't believe them.

steps.m: and even if (connecting to their previous statement)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this to their previous statement about the wolf eating Yusuf. They're saying 'and even if we were truthful' - showing they know their father doubts them.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
The middle weak letter و drops out and the first vowel changes to damma
Adding shadda (doubling mark) makes the meaning stronger and more emphatic
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed the pattern to show who does the action
Added plural ending to show multiple truthful people. This word follows كُنَّا (we were) in the ayah.

steps.m: truthful ones (plural, being acted upon by كُنَّا)
steps.n: Changed the alif to a long alif (ٰ) which is just a writing variation, and added the final vowel mark
The root letters ج-ي-أ changed to جَاءَ (he came) by turning the middle letter ي into a long ا - this is a common change when making past tense verbs
Added و at the end to show many people did the action. Changed the last vowel to ُ to connect with و.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this action with what happened before it, like saying 'and' in English
This is a basic preposition showing position or location. It's one of the core Arabic words that isn't built from a root.
Added an alif mark (ىٰ) at the end to show the correct pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning, it just helps with reading.
Changes from basic root into a word meaning the actual piece of clothing
Added kasra to show this shirt belongs to someone or something
Added هِۦ to show the shirt belongs to him. This word comes after عَلَىٰ (upon) in the ayah.
The weak letter و drops off and we add fatha to create the basic noun. This is a common pattern with words ending in و.
Adding بِ at the beginning shows this blood was used as a tool or means - they came WITH the blood on the shirt
The ٍ at the end connects this word to كَذِبٍ (false) that comes right after it, showing this was false blood they used to trick their father
Changed from verb 'to lie' into noun 'a lie' by adding vowels

steps.m: with a false statement (in this ayah, describing what they brought with the shirt)
steps.n: The tanwin (two kasras) shows this is indefinite (like 'a' in English) and follows the word بِ (with/by) in the ayah
Added vowel marks (fathas) to the root letters to make it mean 'he said' in the past
Changed قَوَلَ to قَالَ by replacing the weak letter و with ا

steps.m: rather (contradicting what Jacob's sons claimed about the wolf)
steps.n: This is a particle that shows Jacob is rejecting his sons' story about the wolf and suggesting a different explanation. It's like saying 'Actually, that's not what happened...'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create past tense verb

steps.m: he really made something seem attractive
steps.n: Adding shadda intensifies the meaning, showing strong deception

steps.m: she really made something seem attractive (subject of your souls/selves)
steps.n: Added feminine marker ت with sukun to show the subject is feminine (referring to أَنفُسُكُمْ - your souls/selves)
Added fatha to help with pronunciation

steps.m: for you all (the ones whose souls made something attractive to them)
steps.n: Added كُمْ which means 'you all'. In this ayah, this refers to the brothers of Yusuf who are being addressed by their father Ya'qub.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun
Plural form made by adding أَ at start and changing middle vowel to ُ - this is a broken plural pattern
Added كُمْ at the end to show possession (your) and ُ before it to show this is the subject doing the action in سَوَّلَتْ (made fair-seeming)
Added كُمْ at end to show it belongs to 'you all'
Root meaning 'command' becomes a noun by making the middle letter silent (with sukun)

steps.m: a matter (object of سَوَّلَتْ - made fair-seeming)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being acted upon in the sentence. This word is what was made fair-seeming in their minds.
Added fatha and sukun to make the noun meaning 'patience'
Added ٌ (tanween) at the end to show this is a noun standing alone

steps.m: so [what is required is] patience (in response to يَقُولُ he says)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this is the response to what was just said. This is part of Yaqub's response to his sons' false story about Yusuf.
Added vowels and ي to make an adjective describing beauty
Added tanween damma to show this is describing the patience (صَبْرٌ) mentioned in the ayah
The word Allah is a proper noun referring to the One God. The shadda (ّ) shows emphasis on the letter ل.
The ُ (damma) at the end shows that Allah is the subject of what's being said in this sentence.
Added وَ at the beginning to mean 'and', and the ٱ (hamzat al-wasl) helps connect the words smoothly when reading.

steps.m: one who is asked for help
steps.n: The مُسْتَ prefix shows someone is asking for help, and the pattern shows they are the one being asked for help (like saying 'the one whose help is sought')

steps.m: one who is asked for help (as the main focus)
steps.n: Added ُ at the end to show this is the main subject being talked about

steps.m: The One who is asked for help (referring to Allah in this context)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ at the beginning to specify this refers to Allah, since He is the ultimate source of help
This is a preposition that shows the blood was upon/on his shirt. In this ayah, it's connecting the fake blood (بِدَمٍ) to Yusuf's shirt (قَمِيصِهِ).
Added the small alif (ٰ) at the end to show the proper pronunciation of the long vowel sound. This doesn't change the meaning.
This is a relative pronoun that connects to the verb after it. In this context, it refers to 'what' they are describing/claiming.

steps.m: to describe or tell about something
steps.n: The root letters show the basic idea of describing something
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed to present tense with ت at start to show 'you' are doing it now
Added ون to show many people are doing the describing. In this ayah, it refers to the brothers describing what happened to Yusuf.
Basic meaning of coming or arriving
Connected letters with vowels and changed middle letter ي to ا. This makes it past tense.
Added ت with sukun at the end to show a female did the action. The alif is written as آ to show it's a long vowel.
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb قَالَ (he said) in this ayah.

steps.m: to move or travel along a path
steps.n: Basic meaning of movement or travel
Doubling the middle letter and adding vowels creates a word for someone who does this action as their main activity
Adding ة at the end turns this into a noun for an object that does the traveling
Adding tanween shows this is referring to any caravan (not a specific one) that happened to find Yusuf
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the start and sukun on first root letter makes the verb causative - showing someone made the action happen
Adding وا changes the subject from singular (he) to plural (they), referring to the group of travelers
Added فَ meaning 'so/then' to show this action followed from the previous event. The travelers sent their water-drawer to get water.

steps.m: to go for water, to arrive at a destination
one who goes for water (object of أَرْسَلُوا)
steps.n: Adding alif after first root letter and vowels makes it mean 'the one who goes for water'. This word is the object of أَرْسَلُوا (they sent) in this ayah.
Adding هُمْ shows it belongs to them. The َ (fatha) before هُمْ shows this word is being acted upon by the verb أَرْسَلُوا (they sent).
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form basic past tense verb
Added hamza at start to show causative meaning (making something happen). The final و changes to ى because it's at the end of the word.
Added فَ to show this action happened as a result of the previous action. In this ayah, this refers to lowering a bucket into a well.

steps.m: to lower something down (especially a bucket)

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to lower' into noun meaning 'bucket'
Added هُ at the end to show possession (his) and َ before it to show this is the object being acted upon in the sentence
Added fathas to make it past tense. This is an intermediate form before the weak letter change.
The و (weak letter) changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs
Added damma and sukun to make it a noun meaning the news itself rather than giving it
Added ى at the end to make it mean 'good tidings' - this is a special way to make the word stronger, like extra good news
Added يٰ at beginning to make it a call of joy or exclamation. The ٰ at the end shows the elongated sound of excitement.
Added fatha to show this is the basic demonstrative pronoun
Added هٰ at the beginning to show we're pointing to something nearby. The little line above هٰ (called alif khanjariyah) is a shorter version of ا
Changes from root meaning to specific noun for a young person
Makes the word indefinite (like saying 'a boy' instead of 'the boy')
Adding أ at the beginning shows someone is making something happen - in this case, making something hidden
Adding وَ at the beginning connects this action to what happened before
Adding وا at the end shows many people did this together
Adding هُ at the end shows who was hidden - in this case, referring to the boy they found (يوسف)

steps.m: to cut into pieces or parts

steps.n: Added short vowels (fat-ha) to make a noun meaning 'a piece' from the root meaning 'to cut'
Changed the first vowel from fatha to kasra to make a noun meaning 'a few'
Added ة to make it feminine, commonly used with numbers 3-10

steps.m: a piece of merchandise (object of وَأَسَرُّوهُ - and they kept it secret)
steps.n: Added the ً (tanween) at the end to show this is the thing being kept secret
This is the proper name of God in Arabic. It comes from the root meaning of divinity and worship, but has become a unique proper noun.
Added shadda on ل to show it is doubled in pronunciation
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject (doer) of the word عَلِيمٌۢ (knowing) that comes after it in the ayah

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of عَلِيمٌۢ/knowing)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this statement with what came before it

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of عَلِيمٌۢ/knowing)
steps.n: Changed alif to hamzat wasl (ٱ) which is a special form of alif used when the word is connected to something before it
Adding the vowels 'a' and 'i' to the root letters to make it mean 'he knew' in the past

steps.m: one who knows completely (as an eternal quality)
steps.n: Added ي between second and third root letters to show this is a permanent quality
Added tanween (double damma) to show this is indefinite, which in Arabic can actually make a meaning more complete or absolute when describing Allah
This combines the preposition بِ (with/by) and the relative pronoun ما (what)
Connected the root letters with fatha to create past tense base form
Adding يَ at start and changing vowels shows ongoing action in present/future

steps.m: they work/act/do (referring to a group in the ayah)
steps.n: Adding ونَ changes it from singular (he) to plural (they)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed ى to وْ to show multiple people did the action. This is a special change that happens with words ending in ى when making them plural.
Added هُ to show who was sold - referring back to Prophet Yusuf (Joseph) who is being discussed in this part of the story

steps.m: and they sold him (connecting to the previous events in the story of يوسف)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this to the previous events - showing this was part of the sequence of what happened to Prophet Yusuf
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the noun meaning 'price'

steps.m: with a price (object of شَرَوْهُ/they sold)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show this is the price they used to sell with
Added tanween kasra to show this is any price, not a specific one
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create a noun meaning the result of reducing value
The tanween kasra shows this is describing the price (ثَمَنٍۭ) mentioned in the ayah, which was insufficient for Yusuf's true worth
Changes from singular coin to multiple coins by adding alif

steps.m: silver coins (as the object being counted in بَخْسٍ معدودة - few/countable)
steps.n: The word changed to show it's being counted (like saying '5 coins'). The middle alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) above instead of a regular alif.
Added مَ at beginning and changed vowels to show this is something that received the action of counting
Added ة to make it feminine, matching with دَرٰهِمَ (dirhams) which is treated as feminine
Added tanwin kasra to show this describes the dirhams mentioned in the ayah
The middle weak letter و changes to ا in past tense hollow verbs
Added وا to show multiple people did the action

steps.m: and they were (connected to فِيهِ مِنَ ٱلزَّاهِدِينَ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what comes after it, showing they were among those uninterested in keeping him
Basic preposition meaning 'in'. This is a particle without a root.

steps.m: in it (referring to selling him for a low price)
steps.n: Added pronoun ه ('him/it') to show what we're inside of. In this ayah, it refers back to the situation of selling Prophet Yusuf for a low price.
Basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added fatha at the end because it connects to the definite word الزَّاهِدِينَ (the uninterested ones). This shows مِنَ is linking to what comes after it.

steps.m: to not care about something or give it up willingly

steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes from describing the action to describing the person who does it
Makes it plural to show we're talking about multiple people

steps.m: the people who don't care about (something) (after مِنَ)
steps.n: Adds 'the' to make it specific and doubles the ز because it comes after ٱل. This word comes after مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah.
Changed to past tense and added 'he' as the doer. The و (waw) from the root becomes ا (alif) because it's in the middle.
Added وَ (wa) at the beginning to connect this statement to what came before it, showing this is part of a sequence of events
Added kasra to show the basic form of this word that means 'one who'

steps.m: the one who (subject of اشْتَرَىٰهُ/bought him)
steps.n: Added ال at the beginning and shadda on the ذ to make it into a relative pronoun. This word connects to اشْتَرَىٰهُ (bought him) in the ayah, showing who did the buying.

steps.m: the one who (subject of اشْتَرَىٰهُ/bought him)
steps.n: Changed the spelling to match Uthmani script: replaced regular alif with wasl alif and ya with alif maqsurah
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
The pattern اِفْتَعَل shows doing something with effort or for oneself. The ت is added between first and second root letters.
The alif at the end shows this is a complete action in the past
Added pronoun هُ meaning 'him' as the object being purchased

steps.m: from (connects مِّصْرَ 'Egypt' to the previous phrase)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects 'Egypt' (مِّصْرَ) to the previous phrase, showing where the person who bought Yusuf was from.
The root meaning evolved to refer to a defined territory or country
Added short i and sukun vowel marks to make the word mean Egypt
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object in the sentence
Adding ة makes this word specifically refer to a female
Adds the preposition 'for/to' before the word
Adds kasra at the end to show it's affected by the 'to/for' before it
Adding ٱ (alif) with sukun makes the word definite (specific woman)
Added هِ ('his') to show possession. The ِ before it connects it smoothly to the word. This word receives the action of قَالَ (he said).
Added ۦٓ which is a pronunciation helper showing the long vowel sound after هِ
Changed to command form by adding أَ at start and changing vowels
Added ى at the end to make it a feminine command

steps.m: to stay or settle in a place

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Added مَ at the beginning to show where the action happens, and changed vowels to show it's a noun
Added the long alif mark (ٰ) to show the extended sound at the end

steps.m: his dwelling place (object of أَكْرِمِى/make honorable)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show it belongs to him. This word is what should be made honorable in the command أَكْرِمِى (make honorable).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Changed the final ي to ى which is common for Arabic verbs ending in a weak letter
Added a small alif (ٰ) above the ى and a pause mark (ٓ) to help with pronunciation
This is a connecting word that joins عَسَىٰٓ (perhaps) with what is hoped for. It works like 'that' in English phrases like 'I hope that...' or 'perhaps that...'
Connected the root letters with fatha to create past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, sukun on first root letter, and damma at end
Added نَا at the end to show who receives the benefit (us)
Makes the 'aa' sound longer when stopping on this word
This is a connecting word that gives a choice between two possibilities: either 'he may benefit us' or 'we may take him as a son'
Basic meaning of taking or grasping something
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb
Added نَ at beginning to show 'we' are doing the action
Added ت with shadda to show we are doing the action for/to ourselves
Added هُ at the end to show who is being taken. The ۥ mark shows this is connected to the next word.
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to form the basic noun for a child.
Added tanween alif (ًا) to show this is the object being taken/adopted in the phrase 'or we take him as a child.' The tanween also shows it's indefinite (a/an).
Added لِ and كَ to the basic demonstrative ذا to make it point to something far away ('that' instead of 'this')
Added كَ at the beginning to show comparison or similarity

steps.m: and like that (connects to مَكَّنَّا - we established)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what comes after it (مَكَّنَّا - we established)

steps.m: to establish, give power or control
steps.n: This is the basic meaning of the root
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Adding shadda (ّ) on middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more emphatic

steps.m: we firmly established (connected to لِيُوسُفَ - for Yusuf)
steps.n: Adding نَا changes 'he' to 'we' and shows this was done in the past. This word connects to لِيُوسُفَ showing who was established.
This is a proper name, likely of Hebrew origin, so we start with the name itself rather than a root. The damma (ُ) on ي shows it's a proper noun.
The prefix لِ shows direction or purpose - like saying 'for Joseph' or 'to Joseph'
The fatha (َ) at the end shows Joseph is receiving the action of the verb مَكَّنَّا (We established/empowered)
This is a basic preposition showing location or containment. It connects with the word الْأَرْضِ (the earth) to show where something is located.
The ي changes to ى at the end of words in Arabic writing. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters together with basic vowel marks to form the noun
Adding ٱلْ makes it refer to a specific earth/ground rather than any earth/ground
The kasra (ِ) shows this word follows the word فِى (in) in the ayah, telling us where something happened
This form turns the action of knowing into the noun knowledge
Adding shadda intensifies the meaning from 'know' to 'teach/make known'
The ن prefix shows 'we' are doing the action
The لِ shows purpose - 'in order to' or 'so that'
The ه at the end means 'him' - he's the one being taught. This word starts with لِ which means 'so that' and shows purpose.

steps.m: and so that we teach him
steps.n: Adding conjunction prefix و meaning 'and'
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it shows that the person who bought Yusuf was from Egypt (مِّصْرَ).
Added the kasra (ِ) mark under م to show how to pronounce it correctly.

steps.m: to return something to its origin/beginning
steps.n: This root carries the basic idea of returning something to its original state or understanding its original meaning
Adding the shadda (doubled letter) shows doing the action intensively - really working to find the original meaning
Changed from a verb (doing the action) to a noun (the thing itself) by adding تَ at start and ي in middle - like changing 'interpret' to 'interpretation'
The kasra at the end shows this word is possessed by or connected to الْأَحَادِيثِ (the narratives/dreams) that follows it
The basic meaning is about something taking place or occurring
Changes from a verb (to tell) into a noun (the thing that is told)
Forms irregular plural by adding أ at start and changing internal vowels
Makes the plural noun definite
Gets an 'i' sound at the end because it follows the word 'interpretation of' (shows possession)
Connected the root letters to form the basic word for deity
Added shadda to emphasize this is THE one true deity, not just any deity
Added damma at the end to show Allah is the one doing the action (being dominant/prevailing) in this sentence

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of غَالِبٌ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this statement with what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense verb (he overcame) to active participle (overcomer) to describe Allah
Adding the two dots on top (tanween) shows this is describing Allah as 'the One who overcomes' - it makes it a complete description
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of, upon, or over something else. Here it connects to أَمْرِهِ (His command/affair) to show Allah's complete control over His affairs.
The alif at the end is written with a special mark (ٰ) called an alif maqsurah, which shows it's a long 'aa' sound at the end of the word
The small alif (ٓ) above shows that we should stretch the sound a bit when reading
Adding the short vowels 'a' (فتحة) turns the root into 'he commanded' in the past
Changed the ending to show this word is owned by or connected to something else
Added pronoun هِ meaning 'his' to show this is Allah's command/affair, since Allah (الله) is mentioned right before this word in the ayah
This is a basic particle used to show contrast or exception between ideas. The ّ (shadda) at the end makes the meaning stronger.

steps.m: and yet/but still (contrasts with previous statement in أَكْرِمِى مَثْوَىٰهُ)
steps.n: Adding وَ at the beginning connects this contrasting idea to the previous statement about honoring Joseph's stay. It shows that even though they planned to honor him, most people still didn't understand Allah's plan.
Added vowels (fatha and damma) to make it mean 'it became many' in the past
Added أ at the beginning and changed vowels to make it mean 'more/most'. This word relates to 'people' (الناس) in this ayah, showing that most people don't know.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb

These are actually two different unrelated words - أَنَسَ means 'to feel comfortable' while نَاس means 'people' (plural of إِنْسَان meaning 'human'). They just look similar but come from different roots.
Adds 'the' (ال) to 'people' and doubles the 'n' sound, owned by أَكْثَرَ
This is a negative particle that makes the following verb يَعْلَمُونَ (they know) into its opposite meaning (they do not know). It's like adding 'not' before a verb in English.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The prefix ي shows this is happening now (present tense) and 'he' is doing it
Adding ون changes 'he knows' to 'they know' (for 3 or more males)

steps.m: they know/understand (referring to most people in لٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ ٱلنَّاسِ)
steps.n: The ون ending shows this refers to a group of people. In this ayah, it refers to 'most people' mentioned right before this word.
Added و at the beginning to connect this clause with what came before it

steps.m: to reach or arrive at something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word shows who received حُكْمًا وَعِلْمًا (wisdom and knowledge) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters and added the shadda to show emphasis on the middle letter
Changed from 'he became strong' to 'was made strong' by changing the first vowel mark to a damma - this makes it passive
Added أَ at start to make it a noun meaning 'maturity/strength' rather than the verb 'was made strong'
Added pronoun suffix to show possession. This word is being acted upon by the verb بَلَغَ (reached)
Changed first letter to hamza with fatha and added alif to make it mean 'give' instead of just 'come'
Added نا at the end to change from 'he gave' to 'we gave'
Added هُ at the end to show who received the action (him)

steps.m: to judge or make wise decisions
steps.n: Connected the root letters together
Added damma and sukun to change the verb into a noun meaning the result of judging - wisdom
Added tanween alif to show this wisdom is what Allah gave to him (the object of ءَاتَيْنٰهُ - We gave him)
Added kasra (ِ) on first letter and sukun (ْ) on middle letter to change from action to noun
Added tanween (ًا) to show this is receiving the action of 'We gave' (ءَاتَيْنٰهُ)

steps.m: and knowledge (object of ءَاتَيْنٰهُ - We gave)
steps.n: Added و with fatha (وَ) to connect this word to حُكْمًا (wisdom) mentioned before it, showing both were given
This is the base demonstrative word meaning 'this/that'. The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) in this style.
Added لِ to make it specifically mean 'that' (pointing to something far)
Added كَ at the beginning to mean 'like' or 'similar to'
Added كَ at the end to mean 'like that' or 'similarly' - كَ here points to something being compared

steps.m: and likewise (we reward نَجْزِى the good-doers)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this to the previous statement. This word connects to نَجْزِى (we reward) that comes after it in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added نَ at start to show 'we' are doing it, and changed vowels to show it's happening now or in the future
The ى at the end is added because this word is connected to المُحْسِنِينَ (the good-doers) who receive the reward
Connected the root letters with vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start to make it mean 'one who' and changed inside vowels to show someone doing the action of being good
Added ين to make it plural masculine
Added ٱلْ to make it definite and َ at the end because it's the object of نَجْزِى (we reward)
The added ا makes the action more intense and shows repeated attempts
Added ت with sukun to show a female did this action in the past
Added هُ to show the object (him) of her action

steps.m: and she tried to persuade/entice him
steps.n: Added و with fatha to connect this to the previous statement
Added kasra under ت to show it's feminine
Added ال at the beginning to make it more specific, like saying 'the one who' instead of just 'who'

steps.m: the one who/which (feminine) (refers to امْرَأَت العَزِيز - the wife of the minister)
steps.n: Changed spelling to Quranic form: ال becomes ٱلّ with shadda, and ي becomes ى. In this ayah, it refers to the wife of the minister (امْرَأَت العَزِيز) who tried to seduce Yusuf.
This is the basic form of the independent pronoun meaning 'he'

steps.m: he (subject of رَاوَدَ - tried to seduce)
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) on ه and fatha (َ) on و to show proper pronunciation. In this ayah, this pronoun refers back to Prophet Yusuf who is the object being seduced.

steps.m: in (location where she was with him)
steps.n: Basic preposition showing location. Not derived from a root. Shows where the action of رَاوَدَتْهُ (she sought to seduce him) took place.

steps.m: in (location where she was with him)
steps.n: The ي changes to ى at the end of words in the Uthmani script. This is just a writing style change that doesn't affect meaning.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to create the basic noun
Added kasra to show this word is connected to what comes after it
Added ها which means 'her'. This word comes after فِى (in) in the ayah, so it means 'in her house'
This is a preposition showing movement or distance away from something. In this ayah, it connects to نَّفْسِهِۦ (himself) to show what Yusuf was being seduced away from.
Added fatha (َ) mark to show the correct pronunciation

steps.m: to breathe, to be precious, to desire

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the noun meaning 'self'
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by someone
Added هِ to show it belongs to him (referring to Prophet Yusuf in this ayah)
Added small alif (ۦ) after هِ as a pronunciation guide
Added shadda for emphasis since this word follows عَن in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
The doubled letter ل shows the action was done multiple times or with intensity
Added ت at the end to show a woman did the action of closing/locking
Added و with fatha at start to mean 'and', and kasra at end to connect with next word (الْأَبْوٰبَ/the doors)
Adding alif makes it a common noun pattern for objects/things
Changed to broken plural form by adding hamza at start and changing middle letter back to و
Added ال to make it specific doors (in this case, the doors of the house mentioned in the ayah)

steps.m: the doors (object of وَغَلَّقَتِ - and she closed)
steps.n: Changed ending to show this is what was closed (the object of the verb closed)
Adding the basic past tense vowels
Changed middle letter و to ا and added ت at end to make it 'she said' instead of 'he said'
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this to what happened before - the woman who owned the house closed the doors, and then she said something
This is the basic form of an exclamatory word used to call or beckon someone, particularly in a seductive way.
Adding the vowel marks turns this into a direct command or exclamation. In this ayah, it's used by Potiphar's wife trying to seduce Prophet Yusuf (AS).
Basic preposition meaning 'for' or 'to'
Added ك at the end to show this is directed to a single male person (Yusuf in this case)
Added fathas to make basic past tense form
