Added تَ at the beginning and ٱ to create an oath formula. This is a special way of swearing by Allah's name, used for emphasis in important statements.
This is an emphasis word that makes you sure about something
The كَ means 'you' (when talking to one man)
This is a basic word that shows something is inside or within something else

steps.m: surely in (connected to إِنَّكَ - indeed you are)
steps.n: The لَ at the start adds emphasis to show certainty. This word connects to إِنَّكَ (indeed you are) to emphasize that someone is definitely in a state.

steps.m: to wander off the right path

steps.n: Changed from a root meaning 'to stray' into a noun showing the act of straying by adding vowel marks (fathas)
Added alif to make it into an abstract concept
The kasra shows this word is owned by something or follows a word like 'in' or 'from'

steps.m: your state of being lost (after في/in)
steps.n: Added كَ to show it belongs to 'you'. This word follows في (in) in the sentence, which is why we see the kasra (ِ) before the كَ.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added ي and changed middle vowel to kasra to show something that has the lasting quality of being old/ancient
Added ٱلْ to make it specific and ِ because it follows في in the phrase لَفِى ضَلٰلِكَ ٱلْقَدِيمِ (in your old error)
Starting with the basic particle لم and adding shadda on م to strengthen its meaning
Adding ا at the end makes it mean 'when' before past-tense actions
Adding فَ at the beginning to show this happens after something else, and extending the final alif (آ) for emphasis

steps.m: that (introduces what happened next in the story)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that introduces what happened next in the story. Here it connects 'when' (فَلَمَّا) to 'the bearer of good news came' (جَآءَ ٱلْبَشِيرُ)
Added short 'a' sounds (fatha marks) to the root letters to make it mean 'he came' in the past tense
Added alif after ج to make it a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changes the verb into a noun describing someone who does this action

steps.m: the one who brings good news
steps.n: Makes it specific by adding 'the'

steps.m: the bearer of good news (the subject bringing news to يَعْقُوب)
steps.n: Adds damma to show this is the doer of the action in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Adding أَ at the start makes the action causative - showing someone made the action happen
Added alif maksura (ى with small alif above) at the end to show this is the correct spelling for this verb type
Added هُ at the end to show what received the action (it)
This is the basic form showing the concept of being high or elevated
Adding ى at the end turns this into a preposition showing something is on top of or upon something else
The small alif (ٰ) is added as a pronunciation guide to show the long 'aa' sound at the end. This word connects to وَجْهِهِۦ (his face) in the ayah, showing that something was placed upon his face.
Changed the root into a noun meaning the physical face
Adds 'of' meaning by adding the -i sound at the end
Added هِ at the end to show the face belongs to him
Added ۦ after هِ to show proper pronunciation of 'his'
The root ردد with doubled last letter indicates repetitive motion
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense meaning
The doubled middle letter with shadda shows strong/intense action

steps.m: he returned to his original state (referring to يَعْقُوب's sight)
steps.n: The ا and ت show someone/something returning back to an earlier state. Here it means Ya'qub's eyes went back to seeing again.

steps.m: then he returned to his original state
steps.n: The فَ shows this happened right after the good news bearer threw something on Ya'qub's face
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changed the pattern to show someone who has this quality strongly
Added an -an ending to make it describe how something is (like saying 'seeing' instead of 'seer')
Added vowel marks to show this is about something that happened in the past
The middle letter و changes to ا because it has a fatha before and after it. This makes the word easier to say.
This particle turns a statement into a question, like adding a question mark in English

steps.m: Did not? (asking about something that happened in the past)
steps.n: Added لَمْ which makes the question about something that didn't happen in the past. In this ayah, it's part of Ya'qub (as) saying 'Didn't I tell you...'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense form
Changed from past tense 'he said' to command form 'say!' by changing middle vowel from 'a' to 'u'

steps.m: I say (connected to لَّكُمْ - to you all)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning to show 'I' am doing the action. The و drops out because it's between two vowels. This word connects to لَّكُمْ which means it's something being said to multiple people.
Starting with the basic particle ل which shows direction or purpose
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger and more emphatic
Adding كُمْ shows this is directed to multiple people being spoken to
This is the basic emphatic particle that adds certainty to statements
The نّ gets a kasra (ِ) to prepare for connecting with a pronoun
Adding ي at the end means 'I' - it shows who the word is talking about
The small alif (ٓ) is added to show the long vowel sound is held slightly longer when stopping on this word
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Adding أ at start changes 'know' to 'make someone know'
Basic preposition showing source or origin
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly with the next word اللَّهِ (Allah). This is a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning.
Added ال to specify this is THE one true deity, and the hamza was dropped because this is a special word used so often
Added the kasra at the end to show this connects to the word مِنَ (from) before it. Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form since it's a special word.

steps.m: what (that you all do not know)
steps.n: This is a question word that asks about things. In this ayah, it's used with تَعْلَمُونَ (you all know) to ask about what they don't know.
Basic negative particle that makes the verb تَعْلَمُونَ (you know) into 'you do not know'
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added تَ at start to show 'you', changed vowels to show present tense

steps.m: you (all) know (responding to أَلَمْ أَقُل لَّكُمْ - 'Did I not tell you?')
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are being addressed. This is part of Yaqub's response to his sons when he says 'Did I not tell you?'
Added vowel marks to make the basic past tense form
The و changes to ا, which is very common in Arabic verbs with و in the middle

steps.m: they said (referring to Yusuf's brothers speaking to their father)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people (Yusuf's brothers) are doing the action
نا shows the father belongs to us (our father)
Changed from 'our father' to 'O father' by adding يا which is used to call out to someone, and removed نا (our)

steps.m: O our father (said by Yusuf's brothers to their father Ya'qub)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show it's 'our father'. In this ayah, Yusuf's brothers are speaking to their father Ya'qub, asking him to seek forgiveness for their sins.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Added است at the beginning to show seeking or requesting forgiveness
Changed from 'he sought' to 'you seek!' by making it a command - notice the different vowels and the sukoon at the end
Added نَا (us) to لَ (for) to show who the action is for. This word connects to اسْتَغْفِرْ (seek forgiveness) to show they're asking their father to seek forgiveness for them.

steps.m: to commit a wrong or sin
a sin (what is being asked forgiveness for)
steps.n: Changed from action to noun. This word refers to what the brothers are asking their father to seek forgiveness for.
Changes from single sin to multiple sins using broken plural pattern

steps.m: our sins (the brothers speaking to their father)
steps.n: Added 'our' to show the sins belong to the brothers speaking
Added alif to lengthen the final vowel sound for emphasis
Adding shadda changes this from a conditional 'if' to an emphatic 'indeed'
Added نا at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. This word connects to كُنَّا (we were) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fathas to make the basic past tense form
Changed و to ا because it's a hollow verb (has weak middle letter)
Changes from 'he was' to 'we were' by adding نا and changing vowel
Added ا after first letter and ِ after second letter to show who does the action
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about many people
Added the fatha mark to show this word is in the object position
Root letters ق-و-ل got vowel marks (َ) to make it 'he said' in past tense. Shows Jacob speaking to his sons.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it has a fatha and comes between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic that makes the word easier to say.
This is a particle that indicates future tense
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation. This word works with the verb أَسْتَغْفِرُ (I seek forgiveness) to show the action will happen in the future - like saying 'I will seek forgiveness'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to show seeking or requesting forgiveness
Changes from 'he seeks' to 'I seek' forgiveness
The ُ shows this is a normal present tense statement
Added fatha to show basic pronunciation
Added كُمْ to show this is directed to multiple people. This word connects to أَسْتَغْفِرُ (I will seek forgiveness) to show who the forgiveness is being sought for.

steps.m: to nurture, care for, raise up

steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures and takes care of others completely
The kasra shows this word is being called upon - like saying 'O my Lord!'
The ى shows this is my Lord. This word is receiving the action of seeking forgiveness in the phrase 'I will seek forgiveness from my Lord'
The َ (fatha) is added to show this word is being used as an object in the sentence
Adding shadda changes this from a conditional 'if' to an emphasis word 'indeed'
Added هُ at the end to show who is being talked about. This connects to the word هُوَ (he) that comes next in the ayah.
Added an elongation marker (ۥ) to show the 'u' sound is stretched when reading. This doesn't change the meaning.
This is a basic pronoun meaning 'he'. In this ayah, it refers back to Allah and connects to the next word الغَفُورُ (The Most Forgiving) as its subject.

steps.m: one who forgives extensively (predicate of هُوَ)
steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun describing someone who forgives a lot. This word describes هُوَ (He) in this ayah.

steps.m: The Most Forgiving One (predicate of هُوَ)
steps.n: Adding ال makes this refer to Allah specifically as THE Most Forgiving One, not just anyone who forgives a lot

steps.m: The Most Forgiving One (predicate of هُوَ)
steps.n: The ُ ending shows this is a predicate (tells us about) the pronoun هُوَ (He)
Added ي between the second and third root letters to show this is a quality or characteristic

steps.m: The Extremely Merciful (One of Allah's names)
steps.n: Added ال to show this is referring to Allah specifically, as one of His divine names

steps.m: The Extremely Merciful (One of Allah's names, described with يَغْفِرُ/He forgives)
steps.n: The ُ shows this word is describing Allah in the context of Him being the one who forgives
This is a basic time particle that shows when something happens. In this ayah, it connects two events: when they entered (دَخَلُوا) and what happened after.

steps.m: Then when (connecting to what happens in دَخَلُوا)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens right after something else. It connects this event to what happened before.
Added fatha marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added وا at the end to show many people (Yusuf's brothers) did this action together
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or over something else. In this ayah, it connects to يُوسُفَ (Yusuf) to show they entered upon him.
Added an alif that shows how to pronounce the end of the word correctly. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added damma marks to show the basic pronunciation of this proper name

steps.m: Joseph (as the object being acted upon by دَخَلُوا - they entered upon)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Joseph is being acted upon - they entered upon him
Root letters أ-و-ي became past tense verb by adding the vowel marks َ (fatha) to mean 'he took shelter'
Changed the word to show causing someone else to have shelter, rather than taking shelter oneself. This word relates to how Yusuf gave shelter to his parents.
Added the alif maksura (ى) with a small alif above it to show this is the end of the word
Basic preposition meaning 'to' or 'towards'
The ى changes to ي with sukun when connecting to pronouns
Added the pronoun هِ (him) which refers back to Yusuf mentioned earlier in the ayah
Adding basic vowel marks to help pronounce the word
Added يْ to show exactly two parents
Added هِ to show the parents belong to him. This word is being acted upon by the verb ءَاوَىٰٓ (took/brought) in the ayah.
Added fatha marks to show it happened in the past. The middle letter و changes to ا, which is common in words with و in the middle.

steps.m: and he said (connects to them entering in the previous phrase)
steps.n: Adding وَ shows this happened after they entered
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he entered' to 'enter!' by making it a command - notice the different vowels and the alif at the start

steps.m: enter! (command to a group) (related to قَالَ - he said)
steps.n: Added plural ending to make the command apply to multiple people. This is what Yusuf said to his family.
Added vowel marks to form a noun meaning a specific territory or region

steps.m: Egypt (as the object of ادْخُلُوا - enter)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Egypt is the object being entered in the phrase 'enter Egypt'
This is a conditional particle that connects two ideas - here it connects to the word شَآءَ (wills/wishes) to say 'if Allah wills'. It shows that what follows depends on Allah's will.
Added short 'a' sounds (fathas) to each letter to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he wanted'
The weak letter hamza (ء) combines with alif to make a long 'aa' sound
This root relates to worship and divinity
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Added ال to make it specific and unique, with special spelling for Allah's name

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of شَآءَ - willed/wished)
steps.n: The word Allah gets a wasl-alif (connecting alif) at the start, a shadda on the lam, and a damma ending to show it's doing the action of willing/wishing
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb about feeling safe
Changed from a past tense verb (he felt safe) to an active participle (someone who is safe/secure) - like changing 'ran' to 'runner'

steps.m: being safe (plural, in state of) (describes how to enter مِصْرَ)
steps.n: Added plural ending to show this describes multiple people. This word describes the state they should be in when entering Egypt (مِصْرَ).

steps.m: he lifted up (subject of the sentence)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb. This word is the main verb showing what Yusuf did.

steps.m: and he lifted up (his parents onto the throne)
steps.n: Added 'and' at the beginning to connect this action with the previous events in the story. This shows the sequence of events as Yusuf honors his parents.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic word for father
Added dual form ending to show exactly two parents
Added هِ to show they belong to him. This word is being acted upon by the verb رَفَعَ (raised) in this ayah.
This is a preposition showing position or location above something. Here it shows Yusuf's parents being raised up onto the throne.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun meaning the thing that is raised up high (throne)
Added ال to specify this particular throne
Added kasra at the end because it comes after على (upon). This shows the throne is receiving the action of being raised upon.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The doubled ر with shadda shows the intensity of falling
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: and they fell down forcefully (to سُجَّدًا/prostrating)
steps.n: Added conjunction و meaning 'and'. This word connects to سُجَّدًا (prostrating) which comes after it in the ayah.
Basic preposition showing direction or purpose
Added pronoun 'him' to show who is being prostrated to in this ayah
Added a small و mark (ۥ) after ه which is just a pronunciation helper, not changing the meaning

steps.m: to bow down with forehead touching ground

steps.n: The ُ (damma) and ّ (shadda) make it plural and show intensity of action

steps.m: bowing down (describing how they fell down to him)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to make it describe HOW they fell down (in a bowing position), instead of WHO was bowing
The root letters ق-و-ل become قَالَ (he said) by changing و to ا and adding fatha marks to show it happened in the past
Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and'

steps.m: to nurture/care for (as a father does)

steps.n: Added fatha to make it a noun meaning father
Added ت at end to make it a special way of saying 'O father'

steps.m: O my dear father! (calling out to يُوسُف's father)
steps.n: Added يٰٓ (ya) at start to show calling out to someone, and تِ at end which is a loving way to say 'my father' - like saying 'dear father' in English. This is how children respectfully call their fathers in Arabic.
This is the basic attention-drawing part that starts many pointing words
The alif is replaced with a small alif above the ه to show it's shortened when connected to what follows
The ذ with fatha is added to show pointing to something specific and near
The alif is added at the end to show this word is the subject doing something in the sentence. Here it's the subject of تَأْوِيلُ (interpretation)

steps.m: to return something to its origin/beginning

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the verb 'returned' into a noun 'interpretation' by adding ت at start and changing middle vowels - shows the result of interpreting (رُءْيٰىَ - my vision)
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of هٰذَا (this is)

steps.m: to see or perceive with eyes or mind

steps.n: The root 'to see' becomes 'vision/dream' by adding ُ (u) at start, changing أ to ء with ْ (stop sign), and adding ا at end to make it feminine
Added ى with ٰ (alif maksura) and َ to show this is my vision/dream. This word is the object being explained (تَأْوِيلُ) in Yusuf's statement to his father.
Basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects with قَبْلُ (before) to show the starting point in time of something.
Root meaning 'face/front' becomes a noun meaning 'before' in time
Adds damma to show this word is in nominative case
This is a special word that emphasizes that something definitely happened in the past. Here it emphasizes that Allah has definitely been good to Yusuf.

steps.m: to make something into something else, to put, to set
he made/put (subject of قَالَ - he said)
steps.n: Added short 'a' sounds (fatha) between the root letters to change it from the basic root into 'he made/put' in the past tense

steps.m: he made it (referring to رُءْيٰىَ - my dream)
steps.n: Added هَا which refers back to رُءْيٰىَ (my dream) mentioned earlier in the verse
The doubled letter shows intensity in meaning
The 'i' sound at the end (kasra) shows this is being used as 'my Lord' - like saying 'O my Lord!'
Added ى at the end to show possession (my). This word is described as being لَطِيفٌ (subtle/kind) in this ayah.

steps.m: to be true, real, or certain

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
Combined the repeated letters with shadda to make a noun meaning the essence of being true

steps.m: truly/really (describes how Allah made Yusuf's dream come true)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to make it describe how something happens. In this ayah, it describes how Allah fulfilled Yusuf's dream exactly as he saw it.
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation of this emphasis word

steps.m: and certainly (connects to جَعَلَهَا - He made it)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before. This word connects to جَعَلَهَا (He made it) in the ayah.
This root carries the basic meaning of goodness, beauty, and excellence
Added vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added أَ at the beginning to show making something good or doing good to someone. In this ayah, Yusuf is talking about how Allah did good to him.
Basic preposition that shows how something is done or connected
Added ي at the end to show this relates to "me"
Added elongation mark (ٓ) to show the long vowel sound is emphasized

steps.m: when (connecting to أَخْرَجَنِى - He took me out)
steps.n: Basic time particle that points to a specific time in the past. Here it connects to when Allah took Yusuf out of prison.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding أَ at the start makes the verb causative - it shows someone is causing the action to happen to something else
Added نِى at the end to show that 'me' is the object being brought out
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or starts. In this ayah, it's used to show Yusuf coming out from prison.
Added fatha at the end because it connects to the next word (السِّجْنِ) which starts with ال. This helps make the words flow together smoothly when reading.

steps.m: prison (a place where people are confined)
steps.n: Changed from action to place - like how 'lock up' becomes 'lockup'
Added ٱل (the) and doubled the س with shadda because it comes after ٱل
Added ِ at the end because it comes after the word مِنَ (from)
Root letters changed to past tense 'he' form by adding vowel marks
Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and'
Added kasra to the preposition ب to connect it with the next word
Added كُم at the end to show this applies to multiple people being addressed
Added fatha at the end to connect this word smoothly to the next word (السِّجْنِ - the prison). This shows Yusuf was taken out from the prison.
Added emphasis by doubling the م with a shadda. This is because the next word starts with ال, which is a common pattern in Arabic.

steps.m: to wander, live nomadically in the desert

steps.n: Changed from a verb (to live in desert) into a noun (desert life) by adding the 'a' sound at start and making middle letter silent
Added ال (the) at beginning and ِ at end because it comes after مِنَ (from)

steps.m: from (starting point of coming out of السِّجْنِ/prison)
steps.n: Basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it shows Yusuf coming out from prison.

steps.m: from (starting point of coming out of السِّجْنِ/prison)
steps.n: Added a small ن (called nun ghunna) at the end to show how it should be pronounced with a nasal sound. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to be far away from something

steps.n: Connected the letters together and added vowel marks to change it from an action into a word about time
The ِ at the end shows this word follows مِنْ (from/after)

This is a connecting word that introduces what happened - in this case, it connects to explain how Satan caused problems between the brothers. It's like saying 'that' when telling what happened in English.

steps.m: to cause trouble or corruption between people

steps.n: Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it mean 'he caused trouble' in the past

steps.m: he caused trouble (subject of the verb, referring to الشَّيْطٰنُ/Satan)
steps.n: Added shadda to emphasize the action. In this ayah, this word shows how Satan caused trouble between Yusuf and his brothers.
Added يْ after first letter with vowels to show someone who does this action intensely
Added ا to make the word refer to someone who is extremely rebellious

steps.m: the satan/devil (referring to a specific one)
steps.n: Added ٱل to make it specific, which causes the ش to double (shown by ّ)

steps.m: the satan (as the one who caused the problem between يوسف and his brothers)
steps.n: Added ُ to show this is the subject doing the action of causing problems between the brothers mentioned in the ayah
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to create the basic preposition form

steps.m: between me (connected to وَبَيْنَ إِخْوَتِىٓ - and between my brothers)
steps.n: Added ى with kasra to show possession by first person (me). This word is part of a compound phrase 'between me and between my brothers' showing the relationship between Joseph and his brothers.
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to create the preposition meaning 'between'
Added و at the beginning to connect this word with the previous بَيْنِى, showing the relationship between 'me and my brothers'
Connected the root letters to form the basic word for 'brother'
Changes the singular 'brother' into a plural form meaning 'brothers'
Added ى at the end to show these are 'my' brothers
Added elongation mark (ٓ) to help with pronunciation
This is a basic emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger or more certain

steps.m: indeed/verily (emphasizes رَبِّى لَطِيفٌ - 'my Lord is subtle')
steps.n: Added the kasra (ِ) under the first letter and shadda (ّ) to show this is a strong emphasis. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Allah being subtle/gentle is definitely true.
Connected the root letters and added shadda to show intensity of the meaning
Added kasra at the end to show 'my lord' or 'lord of' (like saying 'of' in English)
Added ى to show the lord belongs to me (first person). This word is the subject (doer) of جَعَلَ (made) in this ayah.

steps.m: to be gentle and precise in handling things

steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the middle vowel to kasra and added ي to show this is a strong quality
Added tanween dammah to show this is indefinite (a gentle one)
Starting with the basic particles separated: لِ meaning 'for/to' and مَا meaning 'what'
The particles are joined together to form one word. This word connects to يَشَآءُ (He wills) in the next part of the ayah.
Added shadda on ل for emphasis. This is a common strengthening of the ل particle.
The root letters changed to make 'he wanted' - the middle letter ي became ا and got vowel marks
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense and ُ at end to show it's doing the action. This word describes what the Lord (رَبِّى) does in the ayah.
This is an emphatic particle that makes what comes after it stronger or more certain. Here it emphasizes the statement about 'my Lord'.

steps.m: indeed He (referring to رَبِّى - my Lord)
steps.n: Added هُۥ which means 'He' and refers back to 'my Lord' mentioned earlier. The ۥ mark shows this is a long vowel sound.
This is a standalone pronoun that adds emphasis. In this ayah, it refers back to رَبِّى (my Lord) and emphasizes that He is the All-Knowing, the Wise.

steps.m: one who knows everything completely (attribute of Allah)
steps.n: Adding ي between the second and third root letters with the specific vowel pattern creates an intensive form showing complete and perfect knowledge
Added ال to make it definite (the specific one) and ُ to show this describes Allah in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Changes from a past tense verb (he judged) to a noun meaning someone/something full of that quality (wise) by adding ي
Makes the word definite and elevates it to a divine attribute of Allah
The kasra at the end shows possession - that this is 'my' Lord
This form is being used as a call or address to the Lord, adding 'O' to the meaning

steps.m: indeed/certainly (emphasizing ءَاتَيْتَنِى - You have given me)
steps.n: This is a particle that adds emphasis when used before past tense verbs. Here it emphasizes that Allah has definitely given something to the speaker.

steps.m: indeed/certainly (emphasizing ءَاتَيْتَنِى - You have given me)
steps.n: Added sukun (ْ) on the د to show where the word stops
Changed the first letter to hamza with madd (ءَا) to show causing someone else to receive something
Added sukun on last letter to make it a complete past tense verb
Added تَ to show 'you' (masculine singular) did the action in the past
Added نِى to show that 'me' is receiving the action
This is a basic preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or belongs to something
Added fatha at the end to help connect it smoothly to the next word ٱلْمُلْكِ (the kingdom/dominion)
Changed from basic root meaning 'rule' into the noun 'kingdom' by adding the vowel marks ُ (u) and ْ (sukoon)
Added ٱلْ (the) at the start and ِ at the end to show this kingdom belongs to Allah
Doubling the middle letter with shadda shows doing something thoroughly or repeatedly
Adding ت at the end shows you (one person) did this action in the past

steps.m: you taught me intensively (in the past)
steps.n: Adding نِي at the end shows the teaching was done to 'me'

steps.m: and you taught me intensively (in the past) (connected to ءَاتَيْتَنِى/you gave me)
steps.n: Adds وَ (and) to connect this with the previous statement about what Allah gave to Yusuf

This is a particle that connects two words by showing that something comes from or is part of something else. Here it connects 'kingdom/authority' (مُلْك) to 'interpretation' (تَأْوِيل), showing what kind of authority was given.

steps.m: to return something to its original state or explain its deeper meaning

steps.n: Added تَ at start and ي after second letter to make it mean 'the process of interpreting/explaining something deeply'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to الْأَحَادِيثِ (the narratives/events) that comes after it, showing that this is the interpretation of those events
Changes from verb 'to tell' into noun 'speech/narrative'
Changes from single story to multiple stories using a special plural pattern - like how 'foot' becomes 'feet' in English
Makes the noun definite

steps.m: of the narratives/stories (object of تَأْوِيلِ - interpretation)
steps.n: Shows this word is connected to تَأْوِيلِ (interpretation) in the phrase 'interpretation of narratives'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes verb to show who does the action by adding alif after first letter and kasra after second letter

steps.m: O Creator! (being called upon after رَبِّ/my Lord)
steps.n: The word takes the accusative case marker (fatha) because it is being used in a vocative construction after رَبِّ (my Lord)

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated

steps.n: Adds اء to make it a noun referring to something high up
Changed sky/heaven into plural by adding وات (waat) at the end
Added ال to make it 'the heavens' instead of just 'heavens'

steps.m: of the heavens (possessed by فَاطِرَ - Creator)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by فَاطِرَ (Creator/Originator) in the ayah
The root letters get the vowel mark (fatha) to make it mean 'earth' as a regular noun we can use
The kasra (ِ) at the end shows this word follows فِى (in/on) in this ayah
Adds وَ (and) to connect this to السَّمٰوٰتِ (the heavens) mentioned before it

steps.m: you (masculine singular, being addressed by يوسف)
steps.n: This is a pronoun used to directly address one male person. In this ayah, Prophet Yusuf is addressing Allah directly.

steps.m: to protect or be close to someone

steps.n: Adding the vowels 'a' and 'i' turns the root into 'he did it' in the past
Changed from a verb (protected) to a strong noun (protector) by adding shadda
Added suffix to show it's 'my protector'
This is a particle that shows location or position. Here it connects the word ولِىِّۦ (protector) with ٱلدُّنْيَا (worldly life) to show where the protection takes place.
Changed the root into a feminine adjective meaning 'lower' or 'nearer' (compared to the afterlife)

steps.m: the worldly life (contrasted with وَٱلْءَاخِرَةِ - the afterlife)
steps.n: Added 'the' to make it specific and doubled the د letter for emphasis. This word is contrasted with 'the afterlife' in this ayah.

steps.m: to delay or come after something

steps.n: Root meaning 'delay' becomes a describing word for something that is late/delayed
Makes the word feminine by adding ة, changes meaning to refer to 'the last/end part'
Adds 'and' (وَ) at the start + slight spelling change to mean afterlife
Adds 'the' (ال) to make it specific
Shows this word is connected grammatically to دُّنْيَا (worldly life) mentioned earlier in the verse
Changed from root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding fatha vowels
Doubling the middle letter shows the action is done thoroughly and completely
Adding تَ changes it from 'taking something' to 'being taken away' - like when a soul is taken in death

steps.m: take me (in death) (a request to اللَّهِ)
steps.n: Adds نِى meaning 'me' to show who is being taken. This is part of Yusuf's prayer to Allah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to show someone who does the action of submitting, specifically in the context of submitting to Allah

steps.m: as one who submits/surrenders (to Allah) (describing تَوَفَّنِى - take me in death)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes how Yusuf wants to die - in a state of submission to Allah. This connects to the verb تَوَفَّنِى (take me in death) in the ayah.

steps.m: to join or catch up with

steps.n: Adding the vowels 'a' and 'i' turns the root into 'he joined/caught up'
Changed from 'he joined' to 'join!' by adding hamza (أ) at start and changing vowels to make it a command to one person
Added و to connect with previous phrase
Added نِى at the end to show the command is to join 'me' with the righteous ones
Changes the action into a description of someone who has this quality
Makes it refer to multiple righteous people
Adds 'the' and doubles the first letter (shown by ّ) to connect it smoothly with the ال

steps.m: with/among the righteous ones (object of أَلْحِقْنِى/join me)
steps.n: Adds بِ meaning 'with/among'. This word comes after أَلْحِقْنِى (join me), showing who the speaker wants to be joined with.
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby
Added alif mark above ذ to show it's a long vowel sound
Added لِ to change from 'this' (near) to 'that' (far) - like pointing with your finger to something close vs far away

steps.m: that (pointing to something far) (refers to the story of يوسف)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show we're pointing to something specific - in this case, the story of Yusuf that was just told

steps.m: from (connects أَنۢبَآءِ with what comes before)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects 'news/stories' (أَنۢبَآءِ) with what came before, showing these stories come from the unseen.
Root letters got vowels to make the word mean 'news' instead of 'to tell news'
Changed from single news item to plural by following special pattern for broken plurals (أفعال pattern)

steps.m: of the news (plural, possessed by الغيب)
steps.n: Added kasra at end to show this word is possessed by or connected to الغيب (the unseen)
Plural form of 'news' that shows something owns it

steps.m: to be absent or hidden from sight

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to be hidden' into noun meaning 'hidden thing'
Added 'al' to make it specific

steps.m: of the unseen (possessed by أَنۢبَآءِ - news/tidings)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this word belongs to or describes أَنۢبَآءِ (news/tidings)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the basic past tense verb
Added نُ at start to show 'we' are doing the action, and changed vowels to show present tense

steps.m: we reveal it (referring to أَنۢبَآءِ ٱلْغَيْبِ - news of the unseen)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end meaning 'it', referring back to the 'news of the unseen' mentioned earlier in the ayah
This is the basic preposition that shows direction or movement towards something
The ى changes to ي with sukun when adding a suffix

steps.m: to you (referring to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show this is directed to 'you' (masculine singular). In this ayah, it refers to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ as Allah is revealing the story to him.
This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it

Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. In this ayah, it's saying 'and you were not with them' when referring to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ not being present when the brothers of Yusuf were plotting.

steps.m: to be/exist (in a state or place)

steps.n: Added vowels (a-a-a) to make it past tense 'was'
Weak letter و drops between vowels in past tense
Added تَ at the end to show this is talking about 'you' (masculine singular past tense)
This is the basic preposition meaning 'at/with/near'. It's one of the special words in Arabic that doesn't come from a three-letter root.

steps.m: at, with, near (ready to connect to something)
steps.n: The ى changes to يْ to prepare for connecting with the next part. This is like getting a puzzle piece ready to connect.
Added هِمْ which means 'them' (when talking about a group of men or mixed group). This shows who the preposition is pointing to.

steps.m: when (referring to the time of يَمْكُرُونَ - they plot)
steps.n: This word is a time marker that points to when something happened in the past. Here it shows when the plotting took place. It's one of the basic particles in Arabic that doesn't come from a root.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning and changing vowels makes the verb causative - showing someone caused others to do the action
Adding وٓا۟ shows that a group of people (they) did the action together. In this ayah, it refers to Yusuf's brothers agreeing on their plan.

steps.m: to command/order, to deal with matters

steps.n: Adding vowel marks (fathas) turns the root letters into 'he commanded' in past tense
Converting to noun form. This word is what they 'gathered together about' (أَجْمَعُوٓا۟) in the verse.
Adding هُمْ at the end shows this matter belongs to them. This refers to the matter/plan they gathered together about.
Added damma and sukun to show this is an independent pronoun meaning 'they'. This word is the subject of يَمْكُرُونَ (they plot/plan) in this ayah.
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it, like saying 'and' in English.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
The 'ya' at the start shows this is happening now
Adding the masculine plural suffix to indicate multiple people plotting
This is a basic particle in Arabic that can mean different things depending on context
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
The alif is written long (آ) to show emphasis in the pronunciation, but this doesn't change the meaning
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this is about something becoming numerous
The أ at the start shows comparison with others (like adding -er in English)
The ُ ending shows this word is the subject (doer) of the sentence, meaning 'most' of the people

steps.m: to be social or friendly with others

steps.n: Changed from root meaning 'to be social' into the word for 'people' by adding alif and changing vowels
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning, like saying 'all the people'
Added ال to make it specific, like saying 'the people' instead of just 'people'

steps.m: of the people (owned by أَكْثَرُ - most)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this word belongs to أَكْثَرُ (most), making the phrase 'most of the people'
This is a conditional word that usually talks about things that didn't or won't happen

steps.m: and even if (connecting to وَمَآ أَكْثَرُ ٱلنَّاسِ)
steps.n: The و at the start connects this condition to the previous statement about 'most people'

steps.m: to be eager or intensely desire

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb

steps.m: you were eager (addressing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Added تَ at the end to show 'you' did this action in the past. The sukun (ْ) is added before تَ to help pronunciation.

steps.m: to trust, feel secure, or believe

steps.n: Adding long alif at start changes meaning from 'believe' to 'make someone believe' - like turning 'trust' into 'cause to trust'
Changed آمَنَ (to believe) into مُؤْمِن (believer) by adding مُ at start to show 'one who' - this makes it mean the person doing the action
Added ين at the end to show we mean more than one believer

steps.m: with/by believers (connected to حَرَصْتَ - you desired)
steps.n: Added بِ at start to show connection with حَرَصْتَ (you desired). The بِ here means even though you really wanted them to believe, they won't be believers.

This is a basic particle that can mean either 'what' (for questions) or 'not' (for negation). In this ayah, it means 'not' because it's being used with تَسْـَٔلُهُمْ (you ask them) to say 'you do not ask them'

steps.m: and not (connected to تَسْـَٔلُهُمْ - you ask them)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this idea with the previous one, like saying 'and' in English
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Added تَ at start to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense
Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being asked. In this ayah, it refers to asking them about the Quran (عَلَيْهِ)
The ى at the end changes to ي when something is attached to it. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: upon him/it (referring to the Quran mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show who/what is being referred to. Here it refers back to the Quran which was mentioned earlier.

This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it's made of. In this ayah, it connects the word أَجْرٍ (reward) to تَسْـَٔلُهُمْ (you ask them), showing you're not asking them for any reward.
Changes from root letters into a simple noun meaning reward
Added tanween kasra at the end to show this is an indefinite noun (like saying 'a reward' instead of 'the reward'). This word is connected to the word مِنْ (from) that comes before it in the ayah.

This is a particle that has two main uses: 1) 'if' for conditions, or 2) 'not' for negation. In this ayah, it's being used to negate the pronoun هُوَ (it/he) that comes after it, saying that something is not something else.
This is a basic pronoun that means 'he' or 'it' (for masculine things). In this ayah, it refers back to the Quran which was mentioned earlier in the surah.
This is the base form of the exception particle. It shows that what comes after it is different or excluded from what came before.
The shadda (ّ) and alif (ا) strengthen the meaning of exception. In this ayah, it's emphasizing that you're not asking them for any payment - the Quran is ONLY a reminder.
Creates a noun meaning 'remembrance' from the basic meaning 'remember'. This word is what هُوَ (it) refers to in this ayah.
The ٌ shows this is indefinite, meaning 'a remembrance' rather than 'the remembrance'
Forms a noun meaning 'world' or 'realm' - everything that exists and can be known
Makes the word plural to refer to multiple worlds or realms
Adds 'for the' to show this is for all worlds/realms
Adds emphasis with shadda on ل and stretches the ا with ٰ
This is a compound word made from كَ (like) + أَيّ (which) that together mean 'how many'. It's used to ask about large quantities.
Adding ن at the end is a common variation of this word that doesn't change its meaning
Adding و at the beginning connects this phrase to what came before it in the ayah
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from, like saying 'from' in English. It doesn't come from a root.
Added a shadda (ّ) on the م because it comes after the word وَكَأَيِّن. This is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.
Added a sukun (ْ) at the end to show where the word stops. This connects to the word ءَايَةٍ (sign) to show 'from signs'.
The root letters combine with special spelling because of the weak letters. The hamza (ء) replaces the first letter (أ), and the second ي becomes ا.
The ة shows this is talking about one specific sign or miracle
The ٍ shows this word is connected to 'in the heavens' that comes after it in the ayah
This is a basic word that shows location or being inside something. It connects to the word السَّمٰوٰتِ (the heavens) to show what something is in.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This doesn't change the meaning - it's just a writing rule.
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
The root word meaning 'rise up' became a noun meaning 'sky' by changing the last letter و to ء and adding a long aa sound
Changes sky/heaven into plural by adding وات at the end
Adds definite article to make the noun definite

steps.m: the heavens/skies (in relation to وَٱلْأَرْضِ - and the earth)
steps.n: The word 'heavens' is written with long alif marks (ٰ) in this style, and has a kasra (-i) ending to connect it with 'earth' that follows it
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a proper noun
Adding ٱلْ makes it refer to the specific earth we live on
Adding وَ connects this word to the previous mention of heavens (ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ) in the ayah
Adding ِ shows this word is connected to ءَايَةٍ (sign) from earlier in the ayah, indicating 'signs in the earth'
Connected the root letters and added shadda to show doubled letter
Adding ي at the start shows it's happening now
Adding damma at end shows this is the doer of the action

steps.m: they pass by (referring to those who pass by آيَةٍ/signs)
steps.n: Added plural ending to show multiple people doing the action. This refers to those who pass by the signs (آيَةٍ) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on top of'
Added sukun (ْ) before attaching pronoun suffix

steps.m: upon it/her (referring to آيَةٍ 'sign' which is feminine)
steps.n: Added ها which refers back to آيَةٍ (sign) mentioned earlier in the ayah
This is a basic pronoun meaning 'they' for a group of males
The و at the start means 'and', connecting this to what came before. This pronoun is the subject (doer) of the word مُعْرِضُونَ (turning away) in this ayah.
This is a preposition that shows something moving or turning away from something else

steps.m: away from it (referring to آيَةٍ - sign)
steps.n: The ها at the end refers back to the 'sign' (آيَةٍ) mentioned earlier in the verse. This word is connected to يَمُرُّونَ (they pass) to show what they are passing by.
The basic meaning involves turning away from something or presenting something

steps.m: one who turns away (subject of يَمُرُّونَ)
steps.n: Adding مُ at the start and the middle vowel ِ makes this describe someone who does the action. This word describes the people who pass by (يَمُرُّونَ) in the ayah.

steps.m: those who turn away (subject of يَمُرُّونَ)
steps.n: The ون ending makes it plural (more than two males) and masculine. These are the same people who 'pass by' (يَمُرُّونَ) the signs mentioned in the ayah.
This is the basic negative particle مَا that negates the verb يُؤْمِنُ (believe) that comes after it. In this context, it means 'not' rather than its other possible meanings like 'what/that/which'
The و connects this phrase to what came before it, adding 'and' to the meaning

steps.m: to trust, feel secure, believe in

steps.n: Root أمن became آمَنَ by adding ا at start - changes meaning from 'feel secure' to 'actively believe in something'. The ا at start makes it Form IV verb (made someone/something do the action)
Changed to present tense form. This word describes what 'most of them' (أَكْثَرُهُم) do in relation to Allah.
Adding damma at the end to mark indicative mood
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this is a past tense verb describing something becoming abundant
Added أ at beginning to show comparison, like saying 'more' or 'most' in English. The vowels show this specific pattern of comparison.
Added ُهُم at the end to show this refers to 'them'. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb يُؤْمِنُ (believes) in this ayah.
This is the basic word for deity or object of worship
Adding shadda on ل makes this the unique name for The One True God, rather than just any deity
The small diagonal line (fatha) above the letter ل shows how to pronounce it correctly
Adding بِ at start shows how someone believes, and ِ at end shows this is the object being believed in. This connects to يُؤْمِنُ (believes) in the ayah.

This is a basic word used to show that something is different from what was just mentioned. Like saying 'I like all fruits except oranges' - the 'except' shows oranges are different from the other fruits.
Adding ا at the end completes the word. In this ayah, it shows that from 'most of them' mentioned before, something is being excluded.
This is a pronoun that refers to the disbelievers mentioned earlier. It shows who is doing the action in مُّشْرِكُونَ (associating partners with Allah)
The وَ connects this with the previous statement about their belief in Allah, showing contrast between believing in Allah yet still associating partners with Him

steps.m: to make something a partner or equal with something else

steps.n: Added vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he partnered/associated'
The مُ prefix turns this into a noun describing someone who does this action. The sukun and kasra show it's a noun pattern.

steps.m: one who definitely makes partners (with Allah)
steps.n: The shadda (doubled letter) is added because the word comes after a word ending in ن or م, not to emphasize meaning

steps.m: those who definitely make partners (with Allah)
steps.n: The ون ending shows this refers to multiple people (masculine plural)
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added وا to show a group of people did this action
Added فَ to connect this with what happened before

steps.m: Do they then feel safe? (connected to أَن تَأْتِيَهُمْ - that will come to them)
steps.n: Added أَ at the start to make this a question, showing disbelief or criticism of their feeling of safety. The long و (ـُو) emphasizes the group action.

steps.m: that (introduces what might happen in تَأْتِيَهُمْ)
steps.n: This is a particle that connects two parts of a sentence, introducing what might happen. Here it connects to تَأْتِيَهُمْ (come to them) to show what the people might not be safe from.
Connected the root letters together
Added ت at start to show 'she' is doing the action
Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce the word correctly
Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being affected by the coming - 'them'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Changes from a past-tense action (he covered) to describe someone doing the action (one who covers) - like changing 'ran' to 'runner'
Added feminine ending to make it refer to an event or occurrence rather than a person
Added tanween dammah to show it's indefinite and the subject. The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this word.

steps.m: from (connects عَذَابِ to what comes before)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it's made of. Here it connects 'punishment' (عَذَابِ) to what comes before it.

steps.m: from (connects عَذَابِ to what comes before)
steps.n: Added a shadda (doubled م) to help connect smoothly with the previous word when reading
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Long ا after second root letter makes this a noun meaning the result of punishment. This word is in the genitive case because it is possessed by Allah (الله) in the phrase 'punishment of Allah'.
The ِ shows this word belongs to Allah (اللَّهِ) in the phrase 'punishment of Allah'
Changed to the unique name for the One True God by adding ال and merging letters
Added kasra at the end to show this is possessed by the word punishment (عَذَابِ) in the phrase 'punishment of Allah'

This is a connecting word that gives a choice between two possibilities: either a punishment from Allah comes to them, or the Hour comes to them suddenly. It connects the two verbs تَأْتِيَهُمْ (comes to them) in both scenarios.
Connected the root letters together
Added ت at beginning to show feminine subject (referring to غٰشِيَةٌ - overwhelming event), with vowels to show present tense

steps.m: she comes to them (غٰشِيَةٌ comes to them)
steps.n: Added هُمُ at the end to show who is being come to (them)
Connected the root letters with vowels. The middle و becomes ا due to being a weak letter.
Transforms the base into a feminine noun meaning 'hour'
Added ال to make it specific - refers to the Day of Judgment
Added damma to show this is the subject of the verb تَأْتِيَهُمُ (comes to them) in this ayah. Also, the initial alif is written as hamzat wasl (ٱ).
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters and form a basic noun
Forms a noun describing the quality of being sudden

steps.m: suddenly (describing how تَأْتِيَهُمُ ٱلسَّاعَةُ/the Hour comes)
steps.n: Adds -an ending to make it mean 'suddenly' - describes how the Hour comes to people without warning
Added diacritics to show proper pronunciation - damma (ُ) shows it's the subject form of 'they', and sukun (ْ) shows the end of the word
Added و with fatha (وَ) at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb لَا يَشْعُرُونَ (they do not perceive).
This is a negative particle that negates the verb يَشْعُرُونَ (they perceive) that comes after it, making the meaning 'they do not perceive'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they sense/are aware (in لَا يَشْعُرُونَ - they do not sense)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. This word appears with لَا (not) in the ayah to say they are not aware.
Present tense verb meaning 'they perceive/feel' - plural masculine form
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense form

steps.m: say! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak to the people)
steps.n: Changed to a command form by: 1) dropping the middle weak letter و, 2) adding damma on ق, and 3) adding sukun on ل. This is Allah commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak to the people.
Basic demonstrative word meaning 'this'
Added ذا to specify something near to the speaker
Changed ending to هِ to make it feminine, showing we're pointing to a feminine word (سَبِيل/path)
Added small ي (ۦ) as a pronunciation helper
Added ي between second and third letters to make it a noun meaning 'path'
Added ى at the end to show possession (my). This word completes the meaning of هٰذِهِ (this) to say 'this is my path.'
Added elongation mark (ٓ) to show the extended pronunciation of the final ى
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end.
Adding أ at start changes it from 'he' to 'I', with damma on middle letter
This is a preposition that shows direction or movement towards something. In this ayah, it connects the verb 'I call' (أَدْعُوٓا۟) with 'Allah' (ٱللَّهِ) to show who is being called to.
Changed to the proper name for The One True God by adding ال and merging letters
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and shadda on ل. This word is possessed by سُبْحٰنَ (glory/praise) in this ayah.

This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or based upon something else. In this ayah, it connects to the word بَصِيرَةٍ (insight/clear evidence) to show the calling to Allah is based upon clear evidence.
Added an alif symbol (ٰ) to show this is a long vowel sound. This doesn't change the meaning, it just helps with pronunciation.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Changes middle vowel and adds ي to make it mean 'one who sees/has insight very well'

steps.m: a clear insight or evidence (عَلَىٰ in this ayah)
steps.n: Changed from 'one who has insight' to 'the insight itself' by adding ة, showing what the Prophet bases his call on

steps.m: with clear insight/evidence (عَلَىٰ in this ayah)
steps.n: Adds a mark (tanween) to show it comes after عَلَىٰ (on/with)
This is the basic form of the personal pronoun meaning 'I'
Added alif at the end to strengthen the pronoun
Added a small mark (۠) above the final alif to indicate this is a pronoun. This word is the subject of the verb أَدْعُوٓا۟ (I call/invite) in this ayah.
Added kasra at the end to show this word connects to what follows it. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb ٱتَّبَعَنِى (followed me) in this ayah.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it, like saying 'and' in English

steps.m: to follow or go after something/someone

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added اِ at start and doubled the ت to show this is a more intense or deliberate kind of following
Added نِى at the end to show the speaker (Prophet Muhammad ﷺ) is being followed. This word connects to أَدْعُوٓا۟ (I call/invite) earlier in the verse.
Changes verb 'declare perfection' into noun meaning 'glory/perfection'
Added و to connect this phrase with the previous statement about the Prophet's path and followers
Added ال to specify this is THE one true God, and the hamza merged with the lam making it doubled
Added kasra at the end because it follows the word إِلَى (to) in this ayah. The alif at the start changed to a special connecting alif (ٱ).
This is the base negative particle. The alif is written long (آ) to show it's pronounced slightly longer.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English

This is a personal pronoun that means 'I'. In this ayah, it emphasizes that the Prophet ﷺ is the one calling to Allah. The small circle (۠) after the word is just a pronunciation mark showing where to pause if needed.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what group it belongs to. It's like saying 'from' or 'of' in English.
Added َ (fatha) at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word الْمُشْرِكِينَ (the polytheists). This is like a bridge between words to make them flow better when reading.

steps.m: to join or associate partners with something
steps.n: Noun form meaning the act of associating partners with God
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
The مُ prefix and middle ِ vowel change turn this into a noun meaning 'someone who does this action'
The ين ending makes it plural (more than one)

steps.m: the polytheists (those who join partners with Allah)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it specific and َ at the end because it comes after مِنَ (from) in the verse
Base particle مَا that negates the verb أَرْسَلْنَا (We sent) that comes after it
The alif is elongated to emphasize the negation and for proper Quranic recitation
Adding و connects this negation to the previous statement
Adding أ at the beginning makes the action causative - meaning someone caused or made the action happen
Adding نَا at the end changes the subject to 'we'. This is the main verb of the sentence.
This is a basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from. Here it connects with قَبْلِكَ to show 'from before you'
Added vowel marks to the root letters to make a word meaning 'before' in time or place
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by what comes after it (comes after مِن in this ayah)
Added كَ to show this means 'before you'. This whole phrase مِن قَبْلِكَ means 'from before you' or 'before you'
This is a basic exception particle that excludes what comes after it from what came before. It is not derived from a root.
Adding the shadda and alif at the end makes the exception stronger and more emphatic
Changes the word to show multiple men by adding special vowels and an alif
Shows this word is receiving the action of sending (We did not send except men). The ًا ending shows it's the object of أَرْسَلْنَا (We sent).

steps.m: to inspire or reveal divine guidance

steps.n: Added يُ at start to make 'he' form, and vowels to show ongoing action of revealing
Changed from 'he' to 'we' by replacing ي with ن at start
Added emphasis with shadda on ن
Added alif at end to show extended sound
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. It's one of the common Arabic particles that doesn't come from a root.
The ى changes to ي before adding pronouns. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Added هِم which means 'them' (masculine plural). This word is receiving the action of نُّوحِىٓ (We reveal) in this ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. It's like saying 'from' in English.
The م gets a shadda because it comes after a word ending in a vowel (نُّوحِىٓ). This is just for smooth pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to dwell, to settle, to be inhabited

steps.n: Basic noun pattern with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter. This word is in a possession relationship with القرى (the towns/cities), meaning 'people of the towns'.
Adding kasra at the end shows this word belongs to or is connected to the next word (القرى meaning 'the towns')

steps.m: to gather, settle, or dwell in a place

steps.n: Root meaning 'to settle' becomes a noun meaning 'a place where people settle'
Changes from singular 'village' to plural form for a small number of villages
Adds the definite article 'the' and extends the final alif
Added fatha to the question marker أ which turns a statement into a question
Added فَ which shows this question follows from what came before

steps.m: and did they not? (connects to يَسِيرُوا۟)
steps.n: Added لَمْ which makes the question negative. This word connects to يَسِيرُوا۟ (travel) in the ayah, asking 'did they not travel?'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense (he traveled) to present tense (he travels) by adding يَ at the start and changing the ending

steps.m: they travel (in the ayah: so that they may travel)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show multiple people doing the action. This verb comes after فَ (so) in the ayah, making it mean 'so that they may travel'
Basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it connects to الْأَرْضِ (the earth) to show where the traveling happens.
Root letters got short vowels (fat-ha and sukoon) to make a noun meaning 'earth'
Added 'the' at the beginning and 'i' at the end because it comes after the word 'in' (فِي) in the ayah

steps.m: to look at something carefully or think about it

steps.n: Adds يَ at start to make 'he' form in present tense, like saying 'he is looking' instead of just 'look'
Changes from singular 'he' to plural 'they' by adding وا at the end

steps.m: so they may look/observe (connected to يَسِيرُوا۟ - 'they traveled' in the previous phrase)
steps.n: Adds فَ meaning 'so/then' to connect this with the previous phrase about traveling through the land
This is a question word used to ask about the way something happened or the state of something. In this ayah, it's asking about how the end/outcome was for previous nations.
Adding the vowel marks helps show how to pronounce this question word correctly
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense

steps.m: he/it was (describes how عٰقِبَةُ/the end result was)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels in past tense. This is a common change in Arabic that makes the word easier to pronounce.
Added vowels and alif to create active participle pattern
This word is the subject of كَانَ (was) in this ayah, asking about how was the end result of those who came before.
Base word that points to someone or something
Adding ال makes it specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'
Adding ين at the end makes it refer to many people instead of just one
This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. Here it connects with قَبْلِكَ (before you) to show 'from before you'
Changes from root to basic noun meaning 'before'
Adds kasra at end to show this word is being used after a preposition

steps.m: before them (مِن قَبْلِهِمْ = from before them)
steps.n: Added هِمْ to show who or what comes before. In this ayah, it's part of the phrase مِن قَبْلِهِمْ meaning 'from before them'
Changed the root into a noun meaning a place where people gather and move around
Added ُ to show this word is the subject of the sentence
Added و to connect this word to what came before it
Added لَ to emphasize the importance of this home
Added ا after first letter and vowels to create active participle pattern فَاعِل, showing something that comes after or is delayed
Added ة to make it feminine and turn it into a specific noun referring to the afterlife
Added ٱلْ (the) at start and ِ at end to show this describes what kind of home/abode (دَارُ) it is
Root letters get vowels to make the noun meaning 'good/better/best'
Adding the two dots (tanween) at the end shows this is a standalone good thing
This is a connecting word that points back to something mentioned before, like saying 'the one that' in English
Connected the letters together to make one word
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about many people instead of just one
Added ل at the beginning to show these people are receiving something or something is for them
Added vowel marks and shadda (double letter mark) to show how to pronounce it correctly
This root carries the basic meaning of protection and guarding against something harmful
Root letters got vowels (َ) to make 'he protected' in past tense
Added اِتَّ at the beginning to show the action is done to oneself. The ت combines with ق to make تّ (shadda)
Added وْا at the end to show many people did this action
Added ٱ to help pronounce the word smoothly
Starting with the basic negative particle لا which means 'not'
Added فَ which shows one thing follows another, like 'so' or 'then' in English

steps.m: then will you not? (asking about تَعْقِلُونَ)
steps.n: Added أَ to turn the statement into a rhetorical question, connecting to the verb تَعْقِلُونَ (you reason/understand) that follows it
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past
Added ت at start to show 'you' are doing it, changed vowels to show it's happening now
Added ون at the end to show many people are doing this together
This is the basic form of the particle that shows a limit or endpoint
The shadda doubles the ت letter to strengthen the meaning
The ىٰٓ ending shows this word connects to what comes after it. Here it connects to إِذَا (when) to mean 'until when'
This is a time word that shows something happened at a specific moment. In this ayah, it connects to the verb ٱسْتَيْـَٔسَ (despaired) to show when that action happened.
Because إِذَا comes after حَتَّىٰٓ (until), together they mean 'until when' - showing something continued happening until a specific moment
Added vowels to make basic past tense form showing a feeling or state
Added است at beginning to show the complete and thorough nature of losing hope

steps.m: he completely lost hope (subject of جَآءَهُمْ)
steps.n: Changed hamza to hamza on ya due to pronunciation rules. This word shows the complete despair of the messengers before Allah's help came to them.
Changed the first vowel to make it plural, showing more than one messenger
Added 'al' at the beginning to make it specific messengers, doubled the ر with shadda, and added ُ at the end to show these messengers are doing something in the sentence
Added fathas to make it past tense for one person
Combined the double ن into one with shadda and changed last vowel to damma to prepare for plural ending
Added وا at the end to show a group did the action
Added 'and' (و) at the start to connect with what came before
This particle introduces and emphasizes what comes after it, like saying 'indeed' or 'certainly' in English
Adds هُمْ at the end to show this applies to 'they' (referring to the messengers mentioned earlier in the verse)
This particle adds emphasis to the past tense verb كُذِبُوا (they were denied) that follows it. It shows that something definitely happened or was completed.
Added sukun (ْ) on the last letter to show the word stops here. This helps with pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning.
Changed the vowels to show that someone received the action instead of doing it. This is like changing 'hit' to 'was hit' in English.

steps.m: they were lied to/deceived (subject of ظَنُّوٓا۟)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show many people were involved. This connects to ظَنُّوٓا۟ (they thought) in the verse, showing these are the same people.
This is a hollow verb with ي as the middle root letter
Changed root letters to past tense by adding fathas and turning middle letter into a long alif with hamza. Now means 'he came'
Added هُمْ at the end to show who was affected by the action (them)
This is a strong root with no weak letters
Changed from basic root meaning 'help' into the noun 'victory', with damma at end showing it's doing the action of coming
Added نَا at the end to show this victory belongs to us (Allah's victory)
This is a defective verb with و as the last root letter
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to passive form with intensity (doubled middle letter) to show something was done with extra emphasis

steps.m: so was saved (after the help came)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened as a result of Allah's help coming in the previous part of the verse
This is a relative pronoun that refers to people. In this ayah, it shows who was saved, connecting to the verb نُجِّىَ (was saved).

steps.m: to want or wish for something
steps.n: This is a hamzated verb with hamza as the last root letter
Added fathas to make it past tense. The middle letter ي changes to ا, and the final hamza stays
Added ن at beginning to mean 'we' and changed end vowel to ُ to show present tense
Basic negative particle لا with fatha added for pronunciation
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. This word negates the verb يُرَدُّ (is repelled) that comes after it.
This is a doubled verb where the last two root letters are the same
Added vowels to make it 'he returned' in past tense
Changes from past tense 'returned' to present tense 'is returned'
This is a hamzated verb with hamza as the middle root letter

Basic noun pattern formed from the root to indicate the abstract meaning of strength or might. This word is the subject of the passive verb يُرَدُّ (is repelled/averted), showing what is not being averted - Allah's might.

steps.m: our might (subject of يُرَدُّ - is repelled)
steps.n: Added نا at the end to show possession (our). This word is the subject of the passive verb يُرَدُّ (is repelled/averted), so it takes the nominative case.
This is the basic preposition showing separation or distance from something
Added kasra at the end to connect smoothly with the next word الْقَوْمِ (the people)

steps.m: to stand, rise, get up, establish
steps.n: This is a hollow verb with و as the middle root letter
Changes from verb 'to stand' into noun meaning 'people who stand together'

steps.m: the people (acted upon by بَأْسُنَا/our punishment)
steps.n: Added ال for definiteness and kasra because it comes after عَنِ (from/against). This word is receiving the action of بَأْسُنَا (our punishment) being turned away from them.
This is a strong root with no weak letters
Added مُ at the start to show who does the action, and added ِ on the middle letter to complete the pattern
Added ين to show this refers to multiple wrongdoers. This word describes what type of people القوم (the people) are in this ayah.
Added ال to specify these particular wrongdoers, and َ at the end because it comes after عَنِ (from/away from) in the ayah
This is a basic particle that shows something is definite or complete
Added لَ at the beginning to add extra emphasis, like saying 'truly' or 'indeed'
Added fathas to make it past tense. This is the theoretical form before the weak letter change.
This is a common weak letter change in Arabic verbs with و in the middle
This is a basic word that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it shows the lesson is found within their stories.
Added the kasra (ِ) mark under the ف to show how to pronounce it as 'fee'
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make the basic noun form. The repeated ص shows intensity in the meaning.
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being possessed by something
Added هِمْ at the end to show these stories belong to them (a group)
Added the vowel pattern that changes this from an action to a noun meaning
Added ة to show this is one specific instance of learning/crossing over - in this case, a lesson that helps us cross from ignorance to understanding
Added tanween (double damma) to show this is any lesson, not a specific one
This is a special word that means 'those who have/possess' and must be followed by what is possessed. Here it connects to الْأَلْبٰبِ (understanding/intellect).
Added ل to show 'for/to'

steps.m: surely for those who possess (modifies الْأَلْبٰبِ)
steps.n: The shadda (ّ) on ل adds emphasis, showing this is an important statement
Added damma and shadda to make the basic noun form meaning 'mind' or 'intellect'
The word becomes plural to mean multiple minds or intellects
The ال makes it definite (specific minds), and the kasra ending shows it's in the genitive case
This is a negative particle that tells us something did not happen. In this ayah, it's saying that the story was not made up.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show past tense

steps.m: he was (describing what happened in قَصَصِهِمْ their stories)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic to make words easier to say.

steps.m: to tell or narrate something new
a narration, story, or new happening
steps.n: Changes from a verb (to tell) into a noun (the story/narration itself)
Added tanween alif to show this is the object being described as 'fabricated' in the ayah

steps.m: to make up or invent something false

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Added Form VIII pattern with ا at start and ت after first root letter to show doing something intentionally
Changed from 'he made up' to 'it is being made up' by adding يُ at start and changing vowels to show passive voice
Added alif maksura at end to show proper spelling of weak letter ending
This is a basic particle that shows contrast with what came before
Added و at the beginning to connect this contrast with the previous statement
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Doubling the middle letter (adding shadda) changes it from 'being truthful' to 'actively confirming/verifying something'
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'the act of confirming'. In this ayah, it refers to the Quran confirming previous scriptures.
This is the base form of the relative pronoun used to refer to someone or something

steps.m: the one who/that which (describes تَصْدِيقَ - confirmation)
steps.n: Added ال at the beginning which turns into ٱلّ (with shadda) because the following letter is ذ. This makes the word more specific, pointing to something particular rather than being general.
The root letters get vowels (fat-ha and sukoon) to make a word meaning 'between'
The root letters combine to form the basic noun meaning 'hand' - a common body part word in Arabic
Added dual form ending to show exactly two hands
Added sukun to prepare for possession
Added possession suffix to show these are his hands. This word appears in the phrase بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ meaning 'between his hands' or 'before him'.

steps.m: to separate or explain in detail

steps.n: Added a shadda (double letter) to show he did this thoroughly and in detail
Changed the verb into a noun showing the result of the action
Added و to connect this word to what came before it in the verse

steps.m: and a detailed explanation (object of كَانَ)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is the object of كَانَ (was) in this verse
The doubled ل (shadda) shows this means 'completely' or 'totally'. The damma (ُ) makes it into a noun.
The kasra (ِ) shows this word is connected to تَفْصِيلَ (detailing), meaning 'detailing of everything'
The root letters ش-ي-أ become the simple noun شَيْء (shay') meaning 'a thing' by adding the vowels fat-ha and sukoon
Added the tanween kasra (ٍ) at the end to show this thing is owned by كُلِّ (every/all). Also changed the ي to ى which is just a spelling variation.
Changed the vowels and added tanween to make it mean the act of guidance itself. The ي at the end changed to ى because it's at the end of the word.

steps.m: and guidance (connected to عِبْرَةٌ - a lesson)
steps.n: Added وَ to connect this word with the previous concept (عِبْرَةٌ - a lesson) in the ayah

steps.m: to be merciful, to show kindness
mercy (a specific instance of being merciful)
steps.n: Adding ة turns the action of being merciful into the noun 'mercy' - like how 'kind' becomes 'kindness'
Added و at beginning to connect this word to 'guidance' (هُدًى) mentioned before it. The ً (tanween) at the end shows this is one of several things being listed.

steps.m: to stand firm, establish, rise up

steps.n: Changes verb root into a noun meaning 'a group that stands together'
Adds لِّ (with emphasis) meaning 'for/to'
Adds tanween kasra to show this is an indefinite noun that's being acted upon. This word connects to يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe) which comes after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Added يُ at start to make it present tense (happening now), and added ؤ to help pronounce it smoothly
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
